But that’s not the immediate problem; the electrical thunderstorm that is fast approaching is a much more significant concern. 
Mr. Kee rushes into the forest, and he overhears Orsik saying, “don’t be the tallest thing in a field when the storm god Thalos is throwing his bolts.” 
## Group 3rd day of the 3rd tenday of Taraskh

The rain continues as a slight drizzle through the night. The following day, as they march deeper into the forest, it turns into a raging thunderstorm.
Around midday, the storm abruptly stops, and the clouds break. The sun is blindingly hot, making it almost as host as the desert of Anauroch. 
And surrounding the group is a gang of goblins led by a hobgoblin named Mohm. 
Thistle, Orsik and Thuul kils the hobgoblin. The goblins freak out, shoot their arrows, flee into the forest, and hide behind some trees. 
Before the group can attack, the ground sprouts trees and vines that attempt to entangle everyone. Only Thistle can’t escape. 
Thuul rushes into the forest to attack and disappears in a pile of fine dust. 
Orsik stares in shock, “I guess this is why they called it the Forgotten Forest!” 
Thistle is in horror, “This is a Fey Crossing.” 
And then the group hears a voice, “Thistle, is that you?” 
The Goblins in the forest are in shock and start muttering about traitors. 
Mr. Kee kills two with a volley of magic missiles, injuring the other. 
Orsik throws a handaxe killing the last goblin. 
The group stands at the forest edge, worried about the Fey Crossings. Just then, Thuul appears and snaps at the space where the Goblins were. 
“Fey crossings have strange effects. This one sent him six seconds into the future.” 
The voice from the forest shouts, “are you The Thistle Wendrood, related to the Uncle Joon?” 
“What did he do?” 
“Yes, you are indeed his nephew. Only a family member would ask that.” 
Thistle remembers that this must be the sprite in Prince Thornacious’ court that is a spy for the Zhentarim whom Orsik is looking for. 
“Are you part of Prince Thornacious’ court?” 
“My dear boy, don’t you remember Sir Oblivillish? Well, I have heard such wonderful things about you!” He pauses, “from his highness, of course. I was a court member, but it was one of those unfortunate misunderstandings where I went hunting and killed a stag and upset the Prince. He felt that it was his stag. But I was so hungry, having not eaten in two days!” He pauses and looks into the distance, “It was so delicious, and I just wanted to eat it!” 
The group remembers how possessive Prince Thornacious was about his game from when they went hunting and were told to go elsewhere. 
“So what happened,” asks Orsik.
“I was cursed! I can’t turn invisible. I start invisible, then become visible, and then become invisible. A sprite that can not become invisible, the shame and the horror.” 
“Huh?” 
The sprite demonstrates, and it is clear, that the invisibility power no longer works. 
“Yeah, that does appear to malfunction.” 
“So I left the court. It was a lowly court, what with the exile. I know it was forced, even if Prince Thornacious says otherwise. The pompous fool pretends we don’t know Queen Tatiana sent him into exile. He acts as if it was his choice. Well, it wasn’t mine, but the Queen insisted all of the court follow the Prince. She could have sent him alone.” He sighs.
“What is a Prince without his court?” 
Orsik thinks, “my kind of backstabbing lackey.” And then continues, “so you left the court and betrayed your Prince by….” 
The sprite will have none of that, “I was sent into double exile! I was thrown out of the Royal Court and then out of Thornacious’ court. I was dismissed over a terrible misunderstanding.” 
Orsik is thinking, “why don’t I kill this guy.” 
Wallace picks up on Orsik’s intention and tells him to reread the notice from the Zhentarim. 
The dwarf reads it carefully and shouts, “OH, YOU ARE THE SPY THAT I AM SUPPOSED TO PROTECT!” 

# Chapter 15 - Oblivillish the Royal Pixie
## Oblivillish

*There may be a way for me to get out of this exile if I can just convince this Fairy. *
Oblivillish the Royal Pixie, “what’s your plan, Thistle?” 
“Well, we’ll deal with this ettin, and then I suppose I will take you back to Thornacious.” 
“Yes, Prince Thornacious may not be so happy to have me back; after all, there was that misunderstanding about the stag.” 
Oblivillish becomes annoyed when Vardis ask, “well, who killed the stag?”
“I did! But I didn’t know it was his stag.”
Thistle perks up, “he was quite explicit when we met him that all stags belong to him.” 
*“When did the lower classes get permission to question their betters?”*
“Well, maybe I missed that royal memorandum. And it was so delicious.” 
“Have you tried groveling?” 
*“Me grovel?”*
 “It sometimes works with nobles.”
“Well, I have never groveled. I am noble. People grovel to me.” 
“What kind of noble are you? Everyone grovels to a Prince.” 
“Yes, well, that may explain my current exiled status.” Then Oblivillish stares at Thistle as if something came over him, “but you aren’t a noble, and you MUST know how to grovel.” He then pauses again, saying, “I order you to teach me to grovel!” 
Thistle rolls his eyes.
“Perhaps, that wasn’t what I was supposed to do.”
“No.” 
Oblivillish gets on his knees and says, “Is this better?” 
Thistle stares at the awkwardly kneeling noble and sighs, “yes.” 
Oblivillish stands up, and Thistle asks, “Do you know about an ettin named Gralm?” 
“Yes, I worked for him until you killed those goblins. Their death probably ended my employment.” 
“I think not.” Thistle’s eyes twinkle with an idea. “You are the last member of your group that was wiped out by a group of Bad Fruul’s goons. We saved you and killed the goons. And we asked to come to see who your boss was and offer our sword.”
Oblivillish likes the plan and quickly agrees. 

For the next bit little, Oblivillish swallows his pride and attempts to learn the art of the grovel. Wallace, Orsik, and Vardis try hard not to laugh. 
A few hours later, a nearby tree flickers and glows more brightly than the others with an iridescent blue flame. An outline shaped like a door lights up. 
Everyone stares at the door quietly. 
Except for Oblivillish, who says, “That better not be what I think it is… The Forgotten Forest is trying to help us, but why does it always do this? I’ll bet a fistful of fire ants that door leads to a puzzle room. Well, at least it’s probably a shortcut… hopefully not to our inevitable demise.”
The blue flames in the doorway swirl like a distant galaxy. A friendly, toothy maw coalesces and yips enthusiastically before abruptly disappearing.
Unla Kee declares, “I’m going in.” 

Everyone stares at him and then shrugs and follows. 
Unla Kee

Stepping through the gate, first Unla Kee almost tosses his food on the floor. Once nausea passes, he looks around and realizes he is in a cold, fifteen-foot square room with walls made of blue marble and floors made of a plush velvet cushion. 
He hears disembodied giggles, snickers, and low guffaws to make matters creepier.
And then, just as it couldn’t get weirder,  a small dog with a vibrantly-colored mane appears before
The dog yelps excitedly.
Well, it doesn’t quite yelp. In Thistle’s head are the sound of yipping and the dog’s voice. “Travelerrrrs! Travelerrrrs! I knew YOU would come. I know. I RRRREEEU. I have been so bored. But in order to let you through first, we play--word game. I’m learning lots of new words now. Tell me what you seek. At this very very moment!
Unla Kee stares at it. 
“My name is Pandi, and I want to play a game! Can we play a game? CAN WE?” 
Unla Kee nods. 
It then shouts, “what do you want?”
Unla Kee casually observes the wrong guesses of the group. 
Thistle first guesses a grail and then a hallway. Pandi is  very dismissive, “they don’t teach fairies anything these days in fairy school.” Vardis tries, “keep walking through an exit.”
Pandi is becoming frustrated and tries to help them, but they keep failing to figure this out. The fairy creature sighs, “this might take a while. I hope you have food and water.” 
Unla Kee smiles and, like a showman, whispers, “a passage?” 
The dog is delighted and laughs and leaps around the room in joy. Unla Kee stares smugly at the rest of the group. 
“Well done, Unla Kee! But now begins the rrreal fun.”

Pandi bites at the air, and a rainbow of various shapes cascades out of an invisible pocket.
“As rrrue you can, my pawwws arrren’t verrry good fur picking up tiny, tiny rrrobjects. Isn’t that the saaadesst thing everrr? My rrrrooom needs rrrarrrtwork for the rrralls! Will you put these ruetogether to make a picture? For me? Forrr me? For ME? Can you can you?”
Unla Kee stares at the Tangram puzzle and, seeing how Vardis and Thistle eagerly begin to assemble the pieces, joins in.
A few minutes later, the puzzle is solved. The picture scintillates in a hypnotic pattern and attaches itself to the wall. Pandi howls with joy.

“Is this for me? Rrrreally rrrreally? Have I been a good boy? Well, if this gift is for me, then the gift of passage is for you!”
Good luck on your journey!”
With another uncomfortable jolt, the scenery shifts, and Unla Kee is outside again. He looks around and notices some hills. And just over the nearest hillcrest, he can see the rise of smoke and hear the occasional drums of a nearby encampment
## The encampment of Gralm

The group advances to the top of the hillcrest to check out Gralm’s army. And what at an army it is. His encampment can be heard from at least a mile away. Bonfires billow to the sky. Tents, huts, and various ramshackle buildings blend into one another as a sea of humanoids is busy. Countless guards patrol the outskirts of the camp. 
Thistle turns to Oblivillish, “what is the way in? And don’t forget what we said.”
Vardis turns to Thistle, “Do you think he pulls this plan of yours off?”
“He’s a spy. He should be able to deceive some guards.”
“If he’s any good at it!”
Oblivillish snorts, “I am a great spy.” He quickly puts on his goblin attire and snorts, “let’s go.” 
He leads the group to one of the guarded entrances of the encampment. A few minutes later, the guards let him and the group pass. 
As the group walks through the encampment, the camp’s various goblins and hobgoblings glare at them suspiciously. 

Unla Kee notices a hill giant under some construction but decides he must be mistaken.
The guards stop. 
Thistle, Unla Kee, and Vardis stare at the center hut. A hut that is over 30 feet tall. The guard approaches a wall and pulls a flap open to reveal a floor made entirely of skulls. 
As their eyes adjust to the darkness, they notice a ragged wooden throne, and on that throne sits the ettin Gralm. They are wearing simple loincloth and deerskin boots. Gralm stands deliberately.
Orsik notices that he’s a wee bit short for an ettin at only nine feet and is about to make a crack. Still, the presence of the ettin and his four piercing eyes subdues his impulse to be snide. 
“We knew you were coming,” smiles the head with fancifully braided blonde hair. 
“Shh. Who what interrupt quiet! Aargh!” shouts the bald head, fire in his eyes. 
“We want to hear them speak. Elaborate. Tell us why we should not devour you!” 
“No. Less talk. More eat! Hungry! YOU LOUD! MUSIC EVERYWHERE! QUIET!” 
“Patience. We always have patience. Speak your case posthaste,” says the blonde head and waits. 
Thistle prods Oblivillish. 
“Sire, these adventurers saved me from the agents of Bad Fruul.” 
“Interesting, and why should we care?” 
“Because we seek an alliance with you,” says Thistle. 
“An alliance with me?” 
As the conversation progresses, Unla Kee notices that the balding head is suffering from sleep deprivation caused by the Fey. 
Gralm waves his hand, and everyone is silent. “You have made an excellent case for the danger that Bad Fruul presents, and he is indeed an enemy of mine. But what do you suggest?” 
Thistle picks up the SEER’s contract, “we have this contract here that you could sign promising to protect us against our common enemy.” 
“An alliance? Is that the only offer on the table?” 
“Do you have a counterproposal?” 
The bald head shouts, “No more talking. Eat Fairy!” 
Vardis objects, “that is not a proposal we’re willing to make.” 
Unla Kee whispers to his friends, “that guy has a sleep deprivation problem. We should see if we can do anything to help him.” 
Vardis agrees, “wouldn’t hurt.” 
The blonde hair Gralm explains, “my dear friend hates fey music. Since the Fey decided to expand into the Forgotten Forest, their music has kept him up all night.” 
Unla Kee has an idea, “I can help him.” He takes out two pairs of magmin shells with powerful insulation properties and creates two pairs of ettin-sized earmuffs.  

Everyone stares at him as he demonstrates a proficiency none knew he had. 
“I like sound and am very particular to hearing the sounds I want, not the sounds other people want me to hear.” 
The bald head takes the earmuffs and then sighs and falls asleep. 
The blonde-haired head takes the other pair, puts them on, sighs, and then removes them. 
“You have proven yourselves to be enemies of my enemy and friends of mine. But to earn my trust, you must perform feats of strength and drinking!” 
Orsik smiles, “my kind of fun!” 
“To prove your worth, you  must defeat Grindle!” 

A massive ogre, almost 13 feet tall, enters the tent, “I Grindle.” The ogre notices the tiny Fairy and approaches it, “toy for me?” Thistle bristles – “no touching.”
Gralm seeing the situation potentially spiraling out of control commands Grindle and says, “no, they are not a toy for you.” 
Grindle pouts. 
Unla Kee, who recalls that he manipulated the fates twice today, tries to convince Gralm that perhaps they could do this contest tomorrow.
The blonde-haired Gralm thinks and then says, “that would’ve been an excellent idea. Unfortunately, we have already consumed this evening’s food. This is the entertainment. And so, I would hate to be unable to provide entertainment for the camp. They might suddenly become very unreasonable and view the slaughtering of you as a reasonable alternative entertainment. They lack discipline in this matter.”
Unla Kee sighs. 
Gralm claps his hands, and six drinks appear. “You must first drink Pixie Sangria! It is a sickly sweet garnet concoction of my own creation.” 
All six contestants drink, and although they can feel the power of the drink, they hold it down. 
Vardis remarks, “I suppose we can all hold our liquor.” 
Gralm declares, “You must first perform a deadlift of this boar!” 
Everyone stares at Orsik, who walks up, flexes his muscles, says, “I’ve lifted fatter officers over my head,” and then fails to raise the boar.
The group is deflated. 
Thistle tries to get the boar to cooperate by casting – “speak with animals.” The boar agrees to be still, but Thistle cannot lift the creature. 
Vardis and Wallace also fail.
Grindle is about to cheer when Unla Kee has another twinkle in his eye. He casts reduce on the boar, walks up, and picks up the tiny animal.
Grindle howls, but Gralm is impressed.
“The halfling wins!” Gralm shouts. 
The goblins in the hut are impressed. And some are annoyed about their wagers. 
Gralm claps his hand, and six dark ales with hints of orange in tall glasses appear. “Here’s another concoction of my family, The Everyday Ettin.” 
The group all downs their drink. Gralm stares at them as if they expects to see something change but notices nothing. They sighs. 
Gralm then claps his hands again, bringing a brick wall to the tent. 
“You must knock it down with your feet!” 
Unla Kee steps forward and says, “I’ll do it!” 
There is much laughter at the sight of the scrawny wizards. 
“Hey Orsik, I need you to shout on my signal louder than you ever shouted before
Orsik is confused but agrees. 
Unla Kee sits down in front of the wall. All eyes are on him. He stretches his arms and legs and then says, “Watch!” He signals to Orsik, who screams, and his screams distract the folks in the hut just enough to allow Unla Kee to destroy the wall with a thunder wave.
“Bravo!” shouts Gralm. 
Grindle pouts and screams and says, “unfair! Unfair!” 
“The halfling has shown himself to be stronger than he looks!” 
Gralm claps once more, and the destroyed wall is cleaned up. 
He then looks at the group and says, “Now I must introduce you to one of my favorite Ettin classics, The Ettin Grinder. This small glass of crème liqueur is mixed with sour juice and drank immediately.” 
When everyone has a glass, he shouts, “bottom’s up.” 
At this point, Vardis mutters, “I have had hazing rituals for city guards that involved less drinking.” 
Thistle starts to hiccup uncontrollably. 
Gralm looks relieved as if he was expecting something to happen, and now that it did, he is less worried.
“The following contest is a boar dog eating contest. You must eat the maximum number of boar dogs in five minutes. 
Vardis mutters, “yep, I remember doing this at that city as a pledge to join the guards of.” 
But before he can complete his thought, Gralm shouts, “begin.” 
And once again, to everyone’s shock, Unla Kee wins by consuming 24 boar dogs. 
Gralm is delighted and claps his hands. 
Another set of glasses appears, “and here we have my favorite drink, the Harpy’s Dream! This violent frothing substance contains the laments of an entire world in one little shot glass.” 
After the glasses are distributed, he shouts, “bottom’s up!” 
When Gralm notices that no one is affected by this latest drink, he smiles and says.
“We appreciate the proposition you have brought us.”
Just then, the bald head snores loudly. The blonde-haired head glares and continues, “Yes. This arrangement shall be amicable. We know that Bad Fruul’s power wanes as ours waxes. The time to strike is now. We shall challenge and defeat him if you bring the heads of some forest creatures plaguing us.”
“Which?” asks Thistle.
“Why the fey!” 
“That won’t work.” 
“You refuse?” 
“Well, the fey aren’t your enemy.”
The blonde-haired Gralm sighs, “either bring me four heads for my collection or convince the fey to stop playing music near my tent!” 
Thistle is about to protest when Oblivillish whispers, “Prince Thornacious is a fool. He’s been expanding his sphere of control to include the area that Gralm controls. If we don’t stop this music or get some heads, Gralm will not join the alliance against Bad Fruul, and the hill giant will kill the Fey and this two-headed creature.” 
And with that, the group heads back out to the Forgotten Forest. They hope to find the Fey that has been driving the ettin mad. 

## Blasius
Near a small pond surrounded by an open area and a thicket of trees, Blasius, the Satyr, is drinking from a hip flask. A giant eagle with majestic brown and golden wings and an ornamental leaf necklace sits next to him, its head constantly looking towards the sky.
Blasius is startled when he hears a Sprite shout, “that blasted Satyr Blassios is why I was banished from Thornacious’s service! We should attack – now!!!” 
Blasius recognizes Thistle and Oblivillish. He is relieved to see his old friend is trying to protect him. The Fairy stands up, carefully positions himself to grab the angry sprite, and flings himself at Oblivillish. Except Oblivillish was going in the opposite direction. Thistle lands on the forest floor, alone. 
Blasius cringes and is now really worried. But he feels a sense of hope when Orsiktries to tackle the tiny sprite. Still, Oblivillish is trained in the secret art of the Spra-Magaw and deflects the dwarf into the dirt. 
*I’m going to die. Better dead than a chief of staff. *
The killing blow doesn’t come. Blasius opens his eyes and sees a sleeping Sprite with a smug Unla Kee standing behind him. 
Blasius is in shock. Once he recovers his ability to speak, he takes a swig of booze from his hip flask and asks, “what are you doing here?”
Thistle ignores his question, “is Thornacious here?”
Blasius ignores the lack of protocol from the Fairy and then stares at Oblivillish, who is sleeping. “Ox, no! Thees is oblivilliss. No guonder he was so unchappy.”
Thistle prods him to tell, and Blasius confesses his crimes. 
“I was drunk. Which is not surprising. But we must begin at the beginning. Prince Thornacious wants to build a great empire. I just want to go back home. He even destroyed the Fey circle saying we would either build an empire or die trying.” 
Thistle interrupts, “Agreed! And you should build it in a forest that is as grand and glorious as Prince Thornacious!” 
Blasius is annoyed at the interruption and attempts to continue. 
“Yes, but you know.” 
“Pshaw, Blasius. This forest is beneath Thornacious! It is not a PRINCE Thornacious worthy place.”
“I am not the boss who makes these decisions. And you are interrupting me! How often have I told  you that you do not interrupt a Satyr when he is trying to tell a story of tragedy, betrayal, and woe.” He then stops, sighs, and continues. “I have to say what I have to say. My soul requires me to say what I have to say.” He then stares at Unla Kee, “Would you like a drink? I remember you were a strong drinker.” Blasius pours some of his drink into a cup, hands it to the halfling, and says, “Tell me this isn’t the best alcohol you ever had?” 
Thistle rolls his eyes. 
Unla Kee takes a sip and says, “It depends on how much of it you got!” 
Blasius laughs, grabs Unla Kee in a hug, and says, “for you, Mr. Kee, the alcohol will always flow!” 
He then pauses, looks down at his hoofs, and says, “It turns out I was drunk when I told Prince Thornacious that Oblivillish killed a stag.” He starts to cry, “It was a moment of weakness. I was not thinking. How could I do this to my dear friend Oblivillish?” 
Blasius pauses and perks up, “Prince Thornacious does not like me like he used to. But if we could come to an arrangement where Oblivilliss and I did something good for the Prince, he would take Oblivilliss back.” He then stops, “I mean, you do something good for Prince Thornacious that we take credit for.” 
“Well, the greatness and the expansiveness of Prince Thornacius deserves a far grander forest rather than these piddling tiny forests. We have heard of a much larger forest. The high forest with star mountain at its center. That would be a fitting forest for the Prince.” 
Blasius sighs*, “this Fairy is so ignorant of the court customs.”* 
“A good plan. But we need to bring him a gift, and I have just the gift. Some viscious stag-eating carnivores have been in the company of some Unseelie who cheat at cards and sing at all hours of the day.”
Blasius tries to be patient as Thistle tries again to sell the new forest idea. 
And then he realizes, “I must tell them the truth.” 
 “Ever since Oblivilliss was exiled, Thornacious has made me do his work. There are meetings, discussions, action items, more meetings, decisions, recommendations, and follow-ups. I had to keep track of things, Thistle. And I could not drink. And if I forgot anything, I got screamed at. I want Oblivilliss back. And if you kill the Unseelie, I can blame the dead stag on them. I can say that I was drunk and was confused, and Prince Thornacious will take his beloved Oblivilliss back, and I can go back to being a Satyr.” 
Thistle is shocked. “Unseelie here!” 
*“I knew it! I should have started with that!” *
## The Group
Thistle pauses and thinks, “did they bring their warped musical instruments and play their warped Unseelie music?” 
“Yes!” 
“A solution to all of our problems!”

Vardis agrees that the dead Unseelies will solve multiple problems at once. 
 Aha! What do you know about them?” 
Blasius is delighted that Thistle seems interested in dealing with the Unseelie, although he is uncertain why.
And so, while drinking his flask, Blasius leads Thistle and his colleagues to a large clearing. Thistle sees a group of dryads and seedy halfling men and women playing cards with a large pile of coins in the center. 
Orsik counts the coin and readies to attack, but Thistle wants to learn more. Thistle knows that the Fey can not be trusted and wants to ensure these are really Unseelie. 
Near the table, the ranger notices a broken harp that lies in pieces next to one of the dryads. 
Around the table in the clearing are large stuffed animals, lions, tigers, and bears that are rotted with age. 
“Those are the carnivores?” Thistle thinks. 
He finally looks at the Dryads and halfling playing cards. 
“Damn you.” 
“You’re a cheat.” 
“May the false Queen Tatiana fart on you.” 
Thistle is shocked. But he knows patience is better than trusting Blasius. The card players are very focused on each other and the surroundings. 
Blasius turns to him, “See, I told you! The Dryads are Unseelie.”
Oblivillish concurs, “Thistle, as a royal Fey, it is your duty to do as I command and to kill these Dryads.” 
Orsik glares.
“Well, Mr. Orsik, it is true that I wanted Blasius dead, but this way, I can return to my old position with glory. Frankly, seeing my dear friend Blasius die would make me so sad.” 
Everyone else rolls their eyes as they watch Blasius and Oblivillish hug each other calling each other dear friend and brother.
“But still,” Thistle argues, “the dryads are not Unseelie!” 
“My dear boy,” says Oblivillish, “I suppose you are one of those Fairies far removed from the court. Anyone can be an Unseelie. All that is required is to swear fealty to the Gloaming court! As you may have forgotten, the Gloaming court is an organization that swears fealty to the Queen of Air and Darkness and is enemy of the Seelies.” 
Thistle is glum, “But dryads? Dryads? I guess they are playing cards, and cards are evil.” 
“Look, my boy, I want my job back. And I agree with Blasius; these are Unseelie. This Unseelie stench has turned the Ettins against the Fey, who has taken to eating Fey. We must stop the Unseelie before more of our kind die. And Blasius has said they are Unseelie. And finally, anyone who claims to be loyal to Queen Tatiana must attack and kill her enemies.” 
“I guess. I mean, they are enemies of Tatiana.” 
“Queen”
“Queen Tatiana.” 
Orsik is getting progressively more annoyed because his mission is to get Oblivillish back in Thornacious’ good grace, and Thistle is trying hard to not help. 
“Let’s do it!” he shouts.
Blasius turns to Unla Kee – “And you giant fighter?” 
“Just keep the booze flowing.” 
And Blasius hands him another flask. 

# Chapter 16 - Return of the Spy
## The Battle with the Unseelie 3rd day of the 3rd Tenday Of Taraskh
The heroes stare at the Unseelie, and the Unseelie stare back, their game of cards disrupted. 
One of the Dryads, whose leaves and branches remind one of a conifer, casts entangle on the group. The ground around the group sprouts branches and vines. Thistle and Vardis, and Unla Kee are trapped. Orsik and Wallace, however, use their strength to break free. 
The Coniferous Dryad is not entirely pleased; he rushes to a tree nearby and reappears next to another tree some 50 feet away from the group.
“Teleportation!” mumbles the Dwarf.
And then he is hit with a magic missile from the halfling mage, wearing a dirty unseemly robe. 
“Can’t resist that,” he grumbles, “but thank goodness for the buffs of my friend Vardis that absorbed some of the damage!” 
Vardis takes that as a cue and casts a scorching ray aiming at the Confirous Dryad and the Deciduous looking Dryad but misses. 
He grouses, “I can’t move and can’t hit.” 
The Deciduous-looking Dryad calls Orsik, “This isn’t a battle you want to fight in, and we are your friends!” Orsik shockingly agrees and sits down. Thistle shouts, “the damn charm effects of the Dryads, kill that creature, and its charm will be broken!” 
Wallace rolls his eyes, “really? Really?” and then promptly misses. 
Thistle looks at Wallace and Orsik, gives a command to Thuul, and fires an arrow. Shortly after that, the Deciduous Dryad is covered with acid, with an arrow sticking deep into its body. The Dryad can no longer focus on Orsik, and the Dwarf stands up, ready to kill. 
Unla Kee finishes the job with a pair of magic missiles. He directs the last missile at the Coniferous Dryad. 
The Coniferous Dryad recognizing the danger, once again steps through the forest to be close enough to Unla Kee and tells him – “now is a good time to sit down and get a drink.”
The gnome pulls out his flask and begins to drink while mumbling about Blasius and Oblivilish are just sitting and drinking, so why shouldn’t he? 
And then, the creature disappears into the trees and reappears some 40 feet away on the other side of the grove. 
The Unseelie Halfling casts a magic missile again, this time at Wallace. 
Vardis realizing that the entanglement is making it harder to hit their targets casts faerie fire on the Dryad and the Halfling. Only the magic user is affected and is now covered in an eerie pink glow. 
Wallace strikes the halfling with his sword, cutting at the creature deeply. But the Unseelie wizard continues to live. 
Orsik charges at the halfling and throws a handaxe that hits. 

Thistle seeing Unla Kee drinking, pleads with him to get up, but to no avail. The beautiful voice of the Dryad makes his colleague’s voice sound like a screeching bleating whine.   
Just then, a lion and a bear show up. 
Oblivillish shouts – “those creatures ate the stag!” 
Vardis looks at him – “I thought you did?” 
“I was framed. “ 
Vardis understands. 
The Dryad seeing Vardis near the edge of the entanglement, steps into a tree, steps out next to Vardis, swings her staff and misses, and then steps away into the tree. 
Vardis misses as the creature flees, then fails to break from the roots, branches, and vines that hold him in place. 
Wallace, meanwhile, kills the halfling mage. And when the bear charges at him, he dodges the creature’s attacks. 
Seeing the Dryad still alive and his natural hatred of the Unseelie coming to the fore, he commands Thuul to get close and then fires an arrow that misses badly. 
Unla Kee’s drunken stupor ends when he sees the lion charging at him. He raises a finger and casually casts Rhyme’s Binding Ice. The lion escapes the worst of the blast, however. 
The creature stares at Thistle, recognizing him as a Seelie, and pounces. Thistle is dropped, and the lion prepares to tear his throat with his fearsome jaw and gets tangled up in the branches. 
Vardis wonders what to do, “can’t hit anything with my hands caught in these weeds, but maybe …” And his scorching rays hit the lion, killing it, and is even able to wound the bear. Vardis rubs his ring of Correlon. He shouts, “that is how it’s done!” 
Wallace kills the bear and roars as if he had killed Tiamat. Vardis shouts back, “I softened him up for you!” Wallace glares. 
Thistle and Thuul try again to hit the Dryad, and although the creature is covered in acid, it does not relinquish the control of Unla Kee. 
Orsik finishes the job, knocking the Dryad prone on the ground and then cutting it in half with his blade. 
At this point, Oblivillish, freed from the entanglement, flits about and shouts, “I am free!” 
## Return of the Spy

Blasius wakes up from his drunken stupor and asks, “Unla Kee, another drink? Anyone another drink?” He then sees the Dryads dead and shouts, today is a great day! Because today is the day, I am no longer the Chief of Staff of Prince Thornacious.” 
Oblivillish is so happy that he kisses Blasius and then tries to hug Orsik, Vardis, Thistle, and Wallace, who all glare at his unwanted advances. 
Blasius hands Unla Kee a ring of jumping and declares, “To my true honored drinking friend.” And then promptly falls asleep. 
With the Unseelie singers dead, the group heads back to Gralm to get him to sign the contract. Orsik’s mastery of persuasion, the victory in the drinking games, and the magnim sound-canceling headphone are enough to sway Gralm, who announces that he will deal with Bad Fruul. The ettin signs the contract in Grolm’s precise script to cement the deal. 
After signing the contract, he asks, “Will you stay for a small celebration and a drink?” 
Unla Kee stares, “how about a flight?”
“Finally, a civilized adventurer!” Gralm calls his goblin butler, who brings the fine crystals, each filled to the brim with the various brews that the Ettin has manufactured. 
Everyone stares at the gnome, expecting the booze to have some magical effect on him. 
“Some more pleeeze?” 
At this point, Wallace, Orsik, and Vardis are worried. Apparently, some of the effects of the booze can be pretty alarming, so Vardis shouts, “hey buddy, we have some much better booze in our tent!” 
And Unla Kee follows him out. 
In the morning, with their head spinning, the group heads out to meet Blasius and Oblivillish, who is still in the clearing with the dead Unseelie. 
From there, Blasius opens another Fey portal and shouts, “come on!” 
Vardis, worried about the last time they went through a portal and had to solve some puzzles, shouts, “what about the trickster fey?” 
“Not a problem. I am chief of staff. A perk of being chief of staff. The faster way to get there!” 
And with that, the group appears in front of the Prince. 
“What are you doing with this traitorous scum?” 
“Your highness, I was drunk when I made those accusations. Was it a – joke? But thanks to the leadership of Oblivillish and these heroes, we found the true killers: a bear and a lion.”
Prince Thornacious scoffs.
“And there were Unseelie agents that Lord Oblivillish commanded the heroes to attack, and under his leadership, they were defeated! The threat of the Unseelie incursion was averted, and the true killers were found!” 
The Prince turns to Thistle, “is this what happened?” 
Thistle and the rest of the group nod. 
“Blasius, you are now once again my Satyr! And you Oblivillish are once again my chief of staff!” 
Orsik smiles – the mission is complete. 
## The Vision Quest 5th day of the 3rd Tenday of Taraskh
The Satyr is so happy that he steps in front of the group and shouts, “a vision quest, my friends. We will drink and ponder and think!”  
Orsik – “anything is better than doing the bidding of a blind pseudo dragon.” 
The vision quest expands the mind, poisons the liver, and allows our heroes to come into contact with the essential elements of the forest and the Fey. The experience shows them how they can neutralize poison, should they ever be poisoned.
Blasius shouts, “only works once per vision quest.” 
Unla Kee slurs, “sign me up!” 
Six days later, in Parnast, our heroes arrive. 

## Azam 1st day of the 1st Tenday of Mirtul 
*It’s been two weeks since they left. Surely they failed. And the SEER will be disappointed. And I have sat in Parnast for days. The only place to drink is the Golden Tankar*
*Although I must admit the Festival of Greengrass is far more pleasant in a small village than in the desert. Plenty of food, drink and smiling faces and games. Why in the desert, it’s almost a joke to celebrate. *
Azam stares at his drink. He sighs. 
In the distance the sound of small children squealing, “Thistle is coming!” breaks his reverie. 
Azam gets up, stretches out his clothes, brushes off some crumbs, leaves some coin for Ragnar and Elspeth, and walks out into the main square of Old Parnast. 
He shouts, “congratulations! You have secured your rewards!” and hands over a magical potion and a pouch full of gold to Unla Kee.
When Unla Kee does not betray his friends and tells them that the deal he struck was for 200 GP, Azam concludes that perhaps the gnomish wizard could work for the SEER.
He walks up to him, “would you like to join the Lord’s Alliance?” 
The wizard stares at him, “who are they?” 
“Ah yes. The North lacks any central authority to impose order and ensure safety and property laws. Why? Because the North has so many different secret factions, each using its influence and power to advance its agendas. The Harpers are anarchists opposed to a central authority. The Zhentarim are only interested in controlling the trade of any goods and are a parasite on the productive functions of society. The Order of the Gauntlet sees evil behind every stone and is too busy trying to save the world from one evil-doer or another. The Emerald Enclave is devoted to the trees and opposed to productive resource extraction of the lands. It is the Lord’s Alliance that is in favor of order and centralization and safety.”
Azam is convinced the pitch will work.
Unla Kee takes a swig and burps, “no.” 

# Chapter 17 - Big Trouble in Little Parnast
## Orsik 1st day of the 1st Tenday of Mirtul 

As he counts the money from the reward Hsing gave him, he thinks, “this town looks shabbier than the last time we were here.” 
He turns to Vardis, “did this place look this dumpy when we left?” 
Unla Kee yawns, “after a vision quest, everything looks uglier.” 
Orsik shrugs. He pulls out a flower that he had put in his hair,  sniffs it,  sighs, and then looks around more carefully. There are make-shift shelters where strangers live. Animals are in pickets that were hastily set up. 
“Feels gloomy,” he thinks. 
Just then, a member of the militia, called Orsik’s guards, walks up to him, executes a miserable salute that Orsik carefully returns, and then starts talking in a torrent, “Commander Orsik, you know that army we were pretending didn’t exist? Well, it’s a bit more real these days. First, one farm was sacked, and now there are all of these people who have fled into town!” 
“We attack at once.” 
“Um, boss, you might want to talk to Ragnar. He’s been putting together some local defenses in your absence. Not really that successful, but he’s trying.” The guard stares at him, “you’ve been gone for 20 days?”
“A little bit less.”  Orsik notices that he is being judged. Orsik hates being judged and puts a bit of menace into his voice. 
“No judgment, boss. I just thought you could help him. He’s not as good as you at running a town’s defenses. And he’s had some bad luck.” 
Orsik is about to head off when that Fairy asks, “any ettin show up?” 
The guard turns pale, “why would they?” 
Sensing a need to lighten the atmosphere, Orsik shouts, “off to Ragnar the Red.” 
“He’s not red. He’s got brown hair. And his name is Ragnar Redtooh.” 
“See, I was right, Ragnar the Red.”
“There’s nothing red about him.” 
“Well, he’s got a red tooth.” 
“That’s his given name!” 
And then the guard starts to laugh, “Gods, we missed you, Orsik.” 
Orsik pats him on the shoulder and says, “good job, soldier.” 
Orsik then marches to the Golden Tankard. Inside, Ragnar Redtooth, who has brown hair, a large belly, and prominent muttonchops, is bidding three men goodnight. The two are members of the militia that Orsik recognizes, and the third is a short half-elf clad in worn furs.
Ragnar shouts, “get out … my old friends! I need your help!” He turns to the kitchen, “Elspeth, some food.” And she shows up with a plate full of cheese, fresh bread, some oil, a pitcher full of ale, and some mugs. 
“eat!”
After everyone has taken some food, he begins. “Orsik, I have tried my best to fill in your gargantuan shoes to organize a defense since you left. But I’ve encountered resistance and ill fortune at every turn. I suspect foul play.” 

At that moment, Orsik notices Wallace is no longer at the table and sees him outside talking to Elspeth. He smiles. 
Ragnar continues, “it’s Gundalin Cartwright. I will pay 50 gold each for any information you might uncover.” 
“So you want him dead.” 
“no information.” 
“just making sure there is no double entendre.”
“None.” Ragnar pauses and continues, “as a military man Orsik, you know how bad it is when people question your decisions. And every day Cartwright or one of his cronies are questioning a decision of mine.” He sighs, “I need their help. They are the most skilled craftsmen, and without their help, we won’t be able to defend the town.” Orsik notices how the tavern owner looks carefully at Thistle, “they still blame me for the whole Cult of the Dragon nonsense. I saved the town by joining. I was the man working from the inside, but they still refuse to see it that way.” 
Thistle can’t resist, “I wonder why.” And then mutters, “I’ll deal with you later.” 
Ragnar ignores the Fairy and continues talking to Orsik, “If Gundalin is behind the sabotage, I need proof. If he’s not, I need you to reason with him to help me defend the town.” 
Orsik is starting to nod off. 
“Perhaps, I should talk to Vardis?”
## Ragnar 
*Orsik’s a good soldier, but Vardis is whom I need now. I need someone who has brains and judgment, not a strong sword arm. *
“Mr. Vardis, if we don’t get the villagers to work together, all is lost. They will all flee into the hills and be slaughtered.” 

Vardis nods and thinks, “not sure why I care, but I’ll go along with this. There might be some coin, and Orsik seems particularly attached to the safety of this town.” 
“Did you notice that young half-elf Tove? He brought me military intelligence. He said that there are monsters on the move and that the forces are constantly moving, destroying what they find. He claimed he couldn’t find their main camp.” 
The tavern keeper then takes a deep breath, “I’m telling you an attack by Bad Fruul’s army is imminent.”
And then Vardis asks, “what sabotage?” 
He smiles. “First, we have seen tools disappear from our toolshed in the center of town.”
“How often?” 
“A few every day, just to be annoying and slow us down. Not enough to claim a robbery.”
“Second, the day after the southern watchtower was completed, it collapsed, killing a militiaman named Donnal.”
“Explain more?” 
“Well, I am no carpenter, but it was sturdy by my eyes. One of the men thinks he saw the light near the tower’s base just before it collapsed. And after the collapse, the men were convinced it was because we were using materials scavenged from Old Parnast that had been cursed by the Cult of the Dragon. I can assert that there is no more activity of that Cult here.”
“Is that all?”
“No, this morning, the cart we haul materials from Old Parnast broke its second axle in a tenday. The wheels and axel had been replaced by Gundalin.” 
Ragnar is relieved when he hears Vardis say, “we’ll see what we can do.” 
The group

The group brainstorms over what to do next. Unla Kee wants to go check out the shed to see if there is anything good. Orsik intends to stake out the shed, see if anyone steals anything, and follow them. Thistle wants to check out the watchtower and stakeout Gundalin. Ragnar suggests they could talk to the villagers and learn what’s happening. 
As they walk outside the Golden Tankard, Orsik shouts at Wallace that they must get going. Wallace kisses Elspeth and promises to talk later. Wallace rolls his eyes when Orsik explains what Ragnar thinks about his father. “Right, my father is doing his best to betray the town so that Orcs will take over? Really?” 
Just then, Azam runs at them – “I forgot to tell Mr. Underwood where his uncle is.” 
Thistle pauses, “yes?”
“He’s in the town of Triboar. It’s about 400 miles away. And we have more disturbing news.” 
“What did Uncle Joon do this time?”
“The good news is that he did not join the Cult of the Dragon. The bad news is that he joined some secretive organization that even the SEER doesn’t know about. 
Azam knows that Thistle will leave, as will Orsik and Vardis, who want to learn more about their map, which would affect the SEER’s plan, so Azam becomes desperate. 
“The SEER wants me to let you know that the protection of Parnast from Bad Fruul is of paramount importance to the Lord’s Alliance. If you were to successfully defend the town, you would earn the admiration and support of a most powerful personage.”
Thistle, Orsik, and Vardis realize that the town would be destroyed if they were to leave. An earlier Orsik might have left, but this Orsik is loyal to the guards he trained. So Orsik shouts, “this Joon is he going anywhere?” 
“Oh no, he has set up shop, and if he were to leave, the Lord’s Alliance would keep track of him.”
Their conversation is cut short when they overhear villagers screaming in the center of town. Arriving at the scene, they see three afflicted horses; two lay on the ground, one trembling violently, and the other is dead. A third is on its feet, staggering as Eryn tries to steady it. 
Just then, Chandra Stol, the local cleric, arrives and stares. She just stares. 
Thistle examines the horses and concludes that nothing is amiss. “These are inferior beasts of burden to Drakes. Therefore I have a Drake. Nothing to see here. Let’s move on.” 
 Wallace steadies the trembling horse. He then examines the animals further and shouts, “My Gods! The animals must have been poisoned.” Wallace then dramatically announces – “and that one is dead!” 
Wald and Eryn, the two villagers that own the horses, just stare at him and then roll their eyes. Eryn, a childhood chum of Wallace, mutters, “the knowledge one accumulates as an adventurer!” 
Unla Kee then turns to examine the horses, using his standard investigative stance of one eye opened very big and one closed. He stares at the teeth and announces, “there are some evergreen branches in the horse’s mouth and hay!” 
Thistle seeing all of the excitement, uses his medical knowledge to examine the horses and concludes, “yep, inferior beasts of burden. Nothing to see here.” And then pats Thuul on the head. 
Chandra Stol, who has been silent this whole time, squeaks, “my, the adventurers who brought me my temple and saved the village from those raiders!” 
Vardis mutters, “saving horses right now!” 
“I want to help!” 
“Can you cast a spell?”
“Oh no. But Silas’ wife, who passed away, may Kelemvor keep her safe, was a herbalist and may have something that could help.” 
Vardis sneers, “well, I have a spell.” And he casts lesser restoration on the horse on the ground near death. Afterward, he turns to Chandra, “Since I am worried about those assassins, tell me about this herbalist?” 
Thistle is surprised when he sees the dying horse stand up, “strange. I never knew horses could recover from poison like that.” Intrigued, he casts ‘*speaks with animals*’ on the horse to learn what happened. 
“So what happened?” 
The one Vardis healed says - “We ate the hay, and then our stomachs felt gross, so we ate more hay, and then we noticed the hay had a funny taste, so we ate more hay, and then we got sick.” 
The sick one moans, “why shouldn’t we eat more hay? It’s food. Food is always good.” It pauses, “I am not feeling so good,” and then projectile vomits at Thistle, who barely avoids getting drenched. 
Realizing that the vomit didn’t cure the horse, Vardis shouts, “let’s go find this herbalist.” 
At this point, the group takes stock of what’s happening with the rest of the villagers. And realizes that they are in the midst of massive debate. Some argue that they should kill the horse because it’s the plague. Others say this horse was poisoned as retribution for Weld’s actions during the last Greengrass festival. Still, others suggest they rush over to Silas’ garden, tear everything up, and feed it to the horse. Fingers point at individuals, men tsk-tsk, children run around, and in general chaos as every debate, argument, and medical issue of every animal, person and beast are being re-litigated. 
Chandra then asks Thistle, “can you folks do something.” 
Thistle examines the evergreen and asks loudly, “does anyone know anything about that?” 
At which point, the conversation erupts again. Some folks argue that this plant was dragged in by the newcomers. Others say that this plant was placed by someone as a way to scare the newcomers. Still, others shout that this plant is not native to this part of the realm. And at that point, the tension starts to rise as the friction between the refugees and the residents of Parnasts escalates. 
Chandra whispers, “please figure this one out. We can’t have a riot.” 
Orsik can’t resist – “I agree with those who think that it’s a right of every law-abiding citizen of Parnast to carry poison.” 
That then triggers another round of shouting. 
“That poison is the kind of poison we should never allow.” 
“That poison is useful, and only irresponsible people let their horses eat poison.”
“Are you saying Well is irresponsible?” 
“The key to safe poison usage is having access to antitoxins and proper stowage.” 
“Yes, but accidents happen!” 
“This wasn’t an accident.” 
Vardis and Orsik pocket the evergreen as Thistle says, “let’s go check out this herbalist.” 
## Silas Carras
“Why, why isn’t it the Defenders of Parnast, and Mr. Orsik, the head of the militia? You must want some of my *wakeup sauce* for that gnome?” 
The gnome stares at him blankly. 
Silas whispers, “you know, the thing people drink in the morning after having drunk a little bit too much in the evening….” 
“No, some antitoxin for this venomous evergreen, actually,” says Unla Kee. Silas smiles. He’s got a new customer. 
Silas explains for the n-th time that he’s not the herbalist. His dead wife was. And that he has no idea if there is any antitoxin available, but the Defenders are welcome to go look in the backyard. 
Silas laughs as Thistle announces that he will use his survival and nature skills to find this antitoxin, “you’ll need to think through this puzzle,” mutters Silas.  
Silas’ wife liked puzzles, which made it into her garden layout. And to find what you wanted, you had to solve the set of nested enigmas she used to hide herbs from folks who didn’t want to pay.
He knew this would take a very long time. 
But is shocked when Unla Kee hands Thistle the antidote. 
Silas now thinks that maybe these folks can help with another mystery. “Hey, you’re not the only people who found something in this garden. Some of my wife’s bushes were trampled recently.”
Thistle examines the trampled plants and finds a trail he chooses to follow alone that leads out of the garden.  
Silas is about to shout, “what about the horses?” but just then, Unla Kee, who was investigating, stands up and shouts, “the evergreen is Yew, and this is the antidote.”
## Thistle

As  Thistle follows the trial, he overhears the farmer’s joy that their horses are healed. He can also hear that the debate Orsik started about poison rights has not ended. 
“That’s interesting. These footsteps lead right to Gundalin Cartwright’s house. And they lead to the front door.” 

# Chapter 18 - Attack on the Cabin
## 2nd day of the 1st Tenday of Mirtul 

The wyvern howls its morning wake-up call, and with it, the village of Parnast comes to life. 
The adventurers slowly filter into the Tavern of the Golden Tankard to plan their next moves. Wallace has a particular spring in his step and a smile. Orsik and Vardis are rubbing their heads from too much drink. Unla Kee is getting his first drink to settle his nerves. And Thistle is freshly rested after a beautiful eight hours of peaceful sleep. 
“To the cabin?” shouts Ragnar.
Thistle responds, “I suppose so!” 
The rest of the group looks at the overly cheery ranger and nods in agreement. Soon they are following the trail that led to where the mother of Tove Brandimen was supposedly held, hostage. The evidence that Tove had produced the night before supported his claim. 
Vardis and Orsik had had their doubts. 
Vardis had looked at the note supposedly from Kagrota Threeteeth – Mother of Bones - and had whispered to Orsik – “Remember when we pulled the hostage scam?”
Their journey continues for a few hours, in post-drunken or drunken silence, until they crest a hill and spot a cabin amid massive boulders and old fir trees. 
“How pretty,” muse Thistle. “Except for the screaming.” 
Thistle tries to make out what they are saying and gathers that they are human voices. He listens carefully and detects tell-tale slang from the area. And then he blushes and claps his hands over his ears. 
The others turn to him, surprised. 
“Oh my. Those are inappropriate things to be saying.” 
Vardis and Orsik, whose head hurts from the alcohol, decide that there has been enough screaming for one day and charge into the clearing. Thistle shrugs and follows. Wallace mumbles something about first scouting before attacking but follows his mentor. Seeing everyone charge, Unla Kee charges as well. 
Orsik sees a cage in the clearing where humans are trapped. To the east of the cell is a firepit. And behind the pen and the firepit is the Brandimen cabin. 
Orsik sees the prisoners and thinks that if he kills them, the screaming will stop. But he is surprised to discover that the screaming isn’t just some local singing but the human reaction of fear. A goblin is torturing the prisoners with a hot poker.  

Unla Kee, who was following Orsik, is surprised to see Orsik surprised to see Orcs and Goblins. 
The wild charge, unfortunately, was noticed, and the goblins and orcs had taken positions behind cover to ambush the five charging adventurers. 
One of the goblins is wearing a dirty green jacket. The goblin steps behind a shed to the east of the fire pit and fire an arrow at Unla Kee. 
“Why me?” 
Orsik mutters, “because you’re in range and aren’t wearing armor.”
Unla Kee doesn’t think that he should get cover. The goblin, on the other hand, gets behind cover. 
Just then, an orc fires a javelin from the cabin over the prisoners and misses Orsik. 
The orc with a necklace of skulls standing next to the prisoner gets hurt when an arrow from Vardis pierces his armor for 9 HP, and Wallace finishes him off with his sword.
Thistle and Thuul move to attack two goblins. The first goblins thwack at Thuul but cannot hurt the Ranger and his beast. Seeing how ineffectual his scimitar is, the goblin runs. The other goblin fires an arrow and also misses. 
An orc steps out from cover, throws his javelin at the robe-wearing wizard and misses. Unla Kee taunts him – “can’t hit the broadside of a barn!” The orc then runs into cover. Unla Kee, knowing that the multitude of planes are safe only if he takes risks,  refuses to hide.

The goblin with the green jacket, who missed Unla Kee, now tries to hit Orsik. This time his arrow hits the dirt in front of Orsik. 
“Charge!”  shouts the Orc with the necklace of skulls and rushes Orsik. 
The group wonders why, and then the reason is apparent, Kagrotra is screaming orders at her troops, 
“Crush the Puny creatures!”. She is displeased at how her troops have performed. “Do I have to do everything?” she shouts as she hurls a javelin. Unfortunately, the Javelin slips through her fingers. The weapon misses the target and embeds itself in the dirt. 

Vardis reaches for his scorching ray spell and fires at the two goblins that fleed Thistle and Thuul. Only the one who fired the arrow is hit a severe blow.
Wallace, who sees the Ogre within reach, slashes, and misses. 
Unla Kee stares at the battlefield and ponders how to twist fate. He knows exactly what to do and casts Rimes Binding Ice. The ogre is trapped, and the orc standing next to him is not. 
Thistle kills the goblin that attacked him with a scimitar. 
The goblin with the bow hits Unla Kee and shouts, “finally!” 
Unla Kee still refuses to find cover. 
An orc advances on Wallace and Orsik, and his axe attack hits the ground with a great thud. The location of the ground is nowhere near Wallace and Orsik. 
The ogre looks down at his god pleadingly. 
Orsik kills the Orc that had advanced on him. 
The goblin with the Green Jacket steps out from cover and mumbles, “We must kill the wizard!” His arrow hits for 3 HP. 
Unla Kee still refuses to find cover. 
Orsik shouts, “go ahead and try and kill us puny creatures! You can’t even kill an unarmed wizard!” 
But then Kagrota surprises everyone when she casts a spiritual weapon in the form of a hammer and hits Unla Kee. 
Unla Kee, realizing that if he were to flee, the universe would end, stands his ground. 
Vardis tries to help and casts faerie fire, but only one of the orcs is affected. Wallace turns to Unla Kee, who gets the hint and twists the fates to help Wallace strike a critical hit on the Ogre. The Ogre is wounded. 
Unla Kee then points to where he wants Wallace to stand, advances, and casts Aganazzer’s scorcher. The Ogre avoids most of the damage; an orc that had been fighting Wallace doesn’t, and a goblin behind the Ogre dies. 
Orsik had shouted, “get a healing potion,” but was drowned by the burning fire and the screams of death. 
Thistle flies up to get a better angle of attack on the Ogre, reducing Kagrota to 1 HP. She screams, “Fairies! We had a deal with Prince Thornacious!” 
Unla Kee is standing beside an Orc, unarmored, and the Orc misses again. 
The creature stares at his weapon in confusion. Unla Kee stands his ground smugly. 
Orsik kills that orc and then advances on the Ogre. 
Meanwhile, the Greek Jacket Goblin exits the shadows and fires at Unla Kee. And squeals when he sees Unla Kee fall. 
The Ogre attacks Wallace with her spiritual weapon, strikes the flanked Orsik with her hammer, and swings over his head. 
Vardis, who sees Unla Kee on the ground, rushes to his side and heals him. 
Wallace punches the Ogre, who dies. 
Unla Kee, now conscious, rushes up to an orc next to Orsik. He then uses another scorcher spell, killing the Green Jacket Goblin and wounding the Orc. Unla Kee, demonstrating his complete and utter lack of sense of self-preservation, distracts the Orc, who Orsik kills. 
After picking the dead bodies for treasure, the adventurers find scattered coins that add up to 50 gold, and six sapphires worth 100 gold. 
Finally, the group turns to the prisoners once the gold is collected. 
At first, the prisoners speak over each other. The man tries to explain things, and a woman interrupts him and says he’s wrong. And then, finally, another woman explains that they were all part of a commune that Kagrota raided. Bad Fruul had sent Kagrota to destroy the commune, and as a result, several members died.
Orsik and Vardis attempt to express sorrow. 
Thistle, remembering their mission, asks to see their hands to see if any finger is missing. Seeing none, he asks if they have seen a woman matching Tove’s description of his mother. The prisoners shake their heads sadly. One of the women sees the finger and starts to cry hysterically. Her friends console her and explain that that belonged to one of the commune's members. 
Orsik turns to them and asks if they had ever seen a half-elf matching Tove’s description.
One of the women says, “Oh yes! They were thick as thieves with Kagrota.” 
“I told you we should have fed him to the Wyvern.” 
Orsik asks if they know nothing about Bad Fruul.
“Bad who?” 
With that, the group enters the cabin. Inside is a small rustic affair, with animal skins and antlers hanging from the walls. 
“What an ordinary woodman’s cabin,” says Thistle. 
“What a mess,” says Wallace, who almost pukes from the rotting food and discarded trash scattered about the room.
Unla Kee examines a large pile of tools that lay in a haphazard pile in one corner.
Thistle finds a trapdoor under the rug. 
“Classic,” shouts Orsik.
Thistle takes a steep ladder that leads down into a small earthen room beneath the cabin. 
He gasps. The room is not a root cellar. It is far more disturbing. Long multicolored ropes of braided human hair strung with small bells that tinkle as he moves through them hang from the ceiling. 
Orsik and Vardis jump down, and all three notice a crude altar on the far side of the chamber whose top bears the painted image of a black sunburst. 
Two black candles flanking an elven woman’s skull and the shattered remains of a jade statuette are resting atop that stand. Beside the altar lay several chests and bags filled with treasure. 
“Bane,” they both mutter. 
Everyone turns to them, but Thistle, whose Uncle Joon explained everything to him, explains. 
*Bane is a ruthless god of tyranny who believes himself to be the rightful ruler of all the planes and is bent on dominating the Realms. He demands absolute loyalty from his followers and deals harshly with his enemies. When Torm slew Bane during The Time of the Troubles, Cyric absorbed most of his followers. Cyric and his worshipers are chief among Bane’s foes for this crime.  Bane returned in 1372 DR, stronger than before and eager to resume his conquest of the Realms. At that point, many of his former devotees repented and returned to his church. Today his followers can be found everywhere, even among the Lord’s Alliance and the Zhentarim. Worshipers of Bane adhere to a strict hierarchical structure and brook no dissent.*
And with that, the group heads back to Parnast. Foremost on their mind is that  Chandra Stol will be so upset that she has to feed the Wyvern cooked meat instead of living prey. 

# Chapter 19 - All is revealed and plans are set in motion
## Orsik 4th day of the 1st Tenday of Mirtul 

The heroes arrive at the Golden Tankard, tired, bedraggled and exhausted. Ragnar, Gundalin, and Chandra are waiting for them inside. 
“So?” asks Chandra.
“No, mother,” grouses Orsik.
“Charred meat it is.” 
Orsik perks up and requests that the captain of the guard feed the Wyvern. Chandra stares at him, smiles, and, with a gesture of noblesse oblige, tells him to go ahead.
A few minutes later, Orsik stares as the Wyvern uses the thigh bone as a toothpick. The dwarf finds the sight quite relaxing. 
As they walk away, Orsik pats Wallace on the back and says, “someday, you’ll be feeding people to the Wyvern.” 
Wallace stares at him funny. 
As the group heads towards the Golden Tankard, a few local villagers come screaming that a horde of orcs is coming towards the town from the south.  
Then a corporal in the militia appears and shouts, “boss man, I think there is a group of orcs coming from the south. They may have followed you.” 
Orsik pulls his sword out and tells his men to follow him as he sneaks behind the Tavern from the east. He charges when they are all in position, screaming at the top of his lungs. Except there are no orcs. 
“Bossman, I promise you there were orcs!” 
Thistle carefully evaluates the situation and perceives no tell-tale signs of lying – “they were telling the truth about the orcs.” 
Ragnar and Gundalin appear outside, ready to fight the orcs, but they see nothing. As Orsik is about to laugh it all off, he sees an image of an ornate door in his mind with Shou’s writings. He turns to the rest of the group and can tell they see the same thing. 
The door then appears in the wall of the Golden Tankard. 
And then another image pops into his, an idea of him opening the door.
All of his companions are clearly caught up in the same mental image. While he rejects the suggestion, Unla Kee rushes, muttering, “when I stop taking risks, the word ends!” As he opens the ornate door, a small white bird flies out. 
Thistle stares at the bird, thinking it is crucial. The sound of hundreds of birds coming from the octagonal room behind the door convinces him otherwise. 
As Orsik stares at the door, Hsing, the golden blind pseudo dragon, beckons him to go in. 
Orsik refuses.
Hsing conjures up images of death in cities that the Lord’s Alliance protects as a warning to Orsik if he doesn’t enter the door. Orsik shrugs, “maybe if you had been nicer, I would listen to you.” 
Orsik continues to refuse. Vardis and Wallace stay with Orsik. 
Thistle shrugs and heads in. 
The villagers stare at Orsik, who is not entering. And the militia stares at him. Orsik realizes he’s close to doing something he won’t discuss. So, he calls on his militia to follow him. 
Once inside the door, he sees an octagonal room covered by shelves that hold scrolls, books, and birds. One of the militia stares slackjawed at the variety and beauty of the creatures that are calmly preening themselves or eating small dishes of bird seed. 
A large desk takes up a large portion of the room, and on it is a map of the Sword Coast that Unla Kee and Thistle are carefully examining. The map is held down with small marble statues of Shou gods. 
Thistle smiles at Orsik, “this map might help you find your map.” 
Vardis glares, “we have seen many maps. “
The double doors across the entrance open into what appears to be a garden. Standing at the door is a 50’ish-year-old Shou woman wearing a blue robe embroidered with birds. She is holding a silver staff topped with a crane’s head, whose eyes are made of crystal. 
She shouts, “you aren’t in Parnast anymore.” 
Unla Kee grabs a cookie on a silver tray and heads out through the doors. Thistle follows. Orsik stares glumly until she hears him say – “ be a dear, and then grab the tea cookies.” 
Orsik turns to one of his militiamen and tells them to carry the tray. 

## SEER 4th  day of the 1st Tenday of Mirtul 

*Unla Kee came through the door! Perhaps the world’s end is not as close at hand as I feared. But the recent events are cause for alarm. The giants are engaged in a civil war to win favor with their gods. And these battles between the giant tribes will not end well for us all. *
She sighs. 
*Time to do that thing, where I demonstrate the reach of our agents and our information by sharing snippets of information from our files about their private lives. *
Your Uncle Joon is still in Triboar. As for your nemesis Adirond Kroskas, he’s disappeared into the North. And for your beloved Laela, we heard rumors that she lives. One of our agents suggested that some allies of Fire Giants had captured her. 
Thistle stares.
She turns to Orsik and whispers, “I know about the … ” The last word is too indistinct for anyone but Orsik. He turns to her and growls, “that’s not me anymore, and we don’t talk about it.” 
And then smiles at Unla Kee, “I am so happy to see you that you enjoyed the cookies. The world’s end may require that you take significant risks, Mr. Kee. I fear that the entire North may end up in chaos if you and others don’t.” 
Her eyes twinkle, “and I heard you own a tower.” 
Finally, she turns to Vardis, “there is a solution to the map. But part of the fun of a treasure map is trying to figure it out. Here’s what I know: your map’s location is that of an isle that will appear but has not yet.” 
Vardis stares at her, “is that the most useless clue of all time or what?”
*Well, I think that worked. They seem impressed.*
“I have manipulated events for some time, and now I need you to actively participate in my plans.” 
Orsik is about to storm out, but the door is gone. 
“Over the last 30 years, an objective of my organization has been to find Narcellia Varam’myr, a priestess of Lolth who has been trapped for several hundred years in a *headband of intellect. *A follower of Angharradh – the elven deity of spring - named Luistarra imprisoned Narcelia for an attack that destroyed Luistara’s village near Evereska. We had a good lead, but during the rise of the Cult of the Dragon, we lost her and have been unable to divine the location of the headband. We knew it was somewhere near the Greypeak mountains, but where? So we came up with a new plan; we would flush her out by encouraging Chandrfa Stol to seek a replacement for the statue of Angharradh. The statue in Parnast had been defaced by the last owner of the headband. We knew that if it was replaced, she would come back to destroy it. To make that happen, we provided the inspiration for an artist in the Anauoch Desert to create the statue and for Chandra to find him.”
She notices that Vardis wants to interrupt with questions and scowls, “I am not done with the opening remarks. Can you please wait until I am done?” 
She continues, “In parallel, we discovered that there was a way for us to scry out the owner’s location. The magical tomes you provided us from the Marsh of Chelimber provided a ritual that we were able to use it. 
And we discovered that the owner of the headband was Bad Fruul. Narcellia has been urging the hill giant to increase his wealth and girth. She taught him the basics of arcane magic. And with the collapse of the Ordning creating order among the Giants, Narcellia convinced Bad Fruul to step out of his comfort zone and draw monstrous humanoids to his banner. 
Unfortunately, our plots to flush out Narcellia have made Parnast a target. Kragota Threeteeth’s agents and farmers revealed the existence of the new statue. Bad Fruul, urged by Narcellia, has decided to destroy Parnast. 
Worse, we have learned that Chandra Stol has a particular pendant that was owned by her ancestor Luistarra. Narcellia has particularly gruesome plans for the cleric.”
Orsik is appalled. The militia turns to Orsik for what to do. 
Everyone else is wondering what the job is. 
“You have unwittingly helped us create a disaster, putting the village at risk and Chandra Stol. We need you to protect her at all costs, determine when Bad Fruul will attack and defend the city of Parnast, and finally bring me the headband.” 
Unla Kee stares at her dejectedly. 
“And to make it a little bit more worth your while, I offer 600 gold for trying, an extra 200 if no one dies, and an extra 100 if you can keep Chandra safe.” 
At this point, Vardis asks – “what is the Ordning?” 
“The Ordning is the social structure of the Giants that keeps them in check and preserves the peace. When it is dissolved, every Giant tribe and leader will attempt to grow their power at the expense of all other Giants. The goal is to be the new leader after the Ordning is re-established. We do not know who or what dissolved the Ordning. Just that it is dissolved.” 
She then pauses, “some more tea and cookies?” 
Unla Kee takes some nice. 
As the door rematerializes, she walks them to the outside. Just as Unla Kee is about to stop outside, she smiles and says, “here you go.” 

She smiles as he takes the book, “prophecies of the apocalypse.” 
## Vardis
Once through the doors, Vardis turns to his old friend. 
“So what are we going to do, Orsik?” 
“Obviously, protect Chandra, tell her about her pendant, reveal everything. I will make it my personal mission to protect her.”
“I would like to assassinate Bad Fruul.” Vardis stares at him, “I mean not on my own but as a group action.” 
Unla Kee smirks, “I am cool with an assassination.” 
Vardis nods. 
Wallaces, “acts what about protecting the village?” 
Orsik turns to the militia and shouts, “this is what I have trained you to do!” 
“Where?” 
Vardis snorts. 
“Where?” 
Vardis watches as Wallace explains that to the northeast of Paranast is old Paranast. It’s more defensible because it used to be a Zhentarim fortress. 
Vardis stares at his friend; this is the kind of decision he does not excel in.
He turns to his friend, and Vardis says, “well, the old city looks sturdier with walls that can be repaired. The problem is that the locals are convinced the old city is cursed. Don’t forget they felt the towers Ragnar set up burned down because they included logs from the old town.” 
Orsik looks predictably confused, “I need ideas, suggestions?” 
Thistle observes that the old city is a better defensive position.
But Orsik flips out, “tell them to defend the old Zhentarim stronghold and leave their homes?” 
Vardis watches as everyone stares at Orsik, talks to himself, and finally spits out, “we will make our defense here.” 
And then proceeds to give orders to dig trenches, build pickets, and fortify existing buildings. He remembers what happened as he fled one of his jobs and shouts, “build some traps, pits.” 
He turns to Vardis, “I know they will wield their weapons and wear their amour well. But I don’t think I was the right guy to teach them how to make weapons or strategies other than standing and fighting. And then the whole basic first aid was nothing I had to learn since we were friends. And I never had any morale that couldn’t be satisfied with some money. Damn, I forgot to tell them to stock up on food! But I know that my presence will give them a will of iron!”
He stops, “what have I done?”
Vardis says, “the best you could have?” 
Thistle says, “don’t worry, Orsik, I trained their archers well.” 
As they walk away, Orsik hears the sound of a bow breaking.
Vardis turns to him and says, “no, he didn’t.” 
## Chandra Stol 4th day of the 1st Tenday of Mirtul 

Orsik marches right up to the Temple of Axes and calls out to Chandra. When she appears, her pleasure at seeing her fellow bloody thirsty councilman evaporates as Orsik tells her everything the SEER said. 
Chandra pauses and sighs. They* know about this. *
“It is true. Narcellia has plagued my family with her lust for vengeance for centuries. She was responsible for my mother’s death 30 years ago. After that death, I went into hiding and had not heard about the drow. My ancestor Luistara went mad after Narcelia destroyed her village and used hag magic to bound the drow into the headband.”
She pauses and then says, “perhaps there is something I know that might be useful. Narcellia was deathly afraid of the fey, sprites in particular. You are friends of Prince Thornacious, who might be of some use.”  
Chandra then becomes stoic and says, “You can do nothing for me.” 
All but Orsik shrug and walk away. 
Orsik is offended, “what if I’m not good enough for you?”
As Chandra ponders her action, she overlooks Thistle, who leaves the area, goes and gets some herbs from Syla’s garden, and rubs Orsik.  
Everyone stares at him, and he says, “Ms. Chandra Stol, I totally get that you can’t stand his smell, but see these herbs, and to be quite honest, I can’t. These herbs will help.” 
 Orsik glares. Chandra is impressed with his earnestness and Thistle’s willingness to go the extra mile and says yes, only after she asks Unla Kee if the plan makes sense. Unla Kee looks at her and says, “what plan?”
“To follow a group of adventurers into the forest, leaving my home and friends to go and kill my ancient family nemesis.” 
Unla Kee stares at her for a minute as if he is trying to peer into the hidden layers of truth and says, “if you do not come with us, it will mean the end of the world.” 
With that, she agrees. 
## The group

With Chandra in tow, the group ponders its next move. After some discussion, they decide to get help from Prince Thornacious. 
Unla Kee notes that the safest place for Chandra maybe with the fey, but Orsik considers that an insult to Wallace. 
As they are about to go, Sylas tells them of an antidote his wife used to make to cope with the blue mists of the Weathercote Forest. 
Thistle stares at the ingredients and is confused. Orsik just walks away. And Vardis wonders if the herbs can be smoked. 
Fortunately, Unla Kee stares at the ingredients and realizes how everything must be put together. A few minutes later, Unla Kee has made enough for four of them. 
Realizing that one of them will have to go unprotected, Orsik volunteers. 
Before they head out, the group scans the horizon, checks the weather, talks to some locals, ponders the impossible, and concludes that the most likely date to attack is 4 days hence or the 8th day of the 1st Tenday of Mirtul. 

At the forest boundary, Thistle steps forward and says, “I know how to do this.” And he very quickly leads the group to Prince Thornacious’ camp. 
Thistle steps forward and makes his pitch for support. At first, things are going well, and Blasius is eager to help. But then Thistle asks Oblivillish as a hunting master to help him. Oblivillish is outraged and shouts, “We had clarified this point that I am not a hunter! The stags were killed by Unseelie, not by me. You killed those Unseelie to bring these outrageous claims forward again.” 
Thistle tries to clarify, “I didn’t say you killed the Stag, but you are a master hunter!” 
This doesn’t improve things when Prince Thornacious asks, “you are a hunter?” 
“No, sire!” And with that, Thistle is silent.
Prince Thornacious thinks, and Blasius stands and says, “Our highness has decided to help and is sending me and two sprites to fight this Bad Fruul!” 
Chandra Stol seeing that the group is about to leave begs Orsik to convince him to let her stay. Orsik is confused, “so I should stay as well because you need my protection.” She sighs, “no, I just need the fey.” 
Orsik’s pride is hurt, and he asks, “am I not good enough for you?” 

# Chapter 20 - The Spine Ripper Tribe
## Orsik 4th day of the 1st Tenday of Mithul (3 days left)
Orsik stares at Chandra, hurt and confused. 
He then looks at her and tells her, “you don’t know these people. Bad Fuul can’t come here personally, but he could send someone here to kill you.” 
Sensing he’s got a winning argument, he continues, “he doesn’t have to kill you on his own. Why would he do that? He’s evil over Lord minion. He has minions to do his evil work for him, so he’s just gonna send someone here. Well, you’re all alone. These people are not gonna be able to protect you.” 
Seeing the winning argument right in front of Orsik, he shouts, “I’m the only one who can guarantee your safety.”
Chandra is shocked, intimidated by his plea’s forcefulness, and says, “yes.” 
Blasius turns to Thistle, “guell my boy! I am off.”
Orsik is about to follow him when Prince Thornacious shouts, “a fine dinner for our friends.”
## The group 5th day of the 1st Tenday of Mithul (2 days left)
The following day the heroes decide to see if they can find where Bad Fruul is. To maximize their effort,s they split up and promise to return later that day to Parnast to reveal what they have learned.
Thistle finds evidence of how Bad Fruul intends to invade Parnast by doing an aerial survey. Vardis later confirms the route by carefully investigating the forest trails near Parnast. Wallace, leveraging his local knowledge, further confirms the most likely direction. 
Thistle, Vardis, and Wallace confer and agree that the attack will come from the north. Then all three inspect the fortifications and are pleased to see the town center with the roads barricaded and pits dug in front of the barricades. 
Orsik finds some goblins that he deceives with information about Bad Fruul. The goblins reflect on what they have learned, and fewer join the hill giant’s banner. 
Unla Kee, for his part, recognizes the banner of Bad Fruul in the hands of some hoard members. From that, he extrapolates the size of the hoard. And from that, he infers that a tribe of Orcs has not yet joined the giant’s army. And from his knowledge of the area, he guesses the name, “The Spinerippers,” and a location for the orcs.

## The group – 6th day of the 1st Tenday of Mithul (2 days left)

In the morning, the companions head out to find the orcs’ den. Thistle shouts, “I got this!” and leads them straight to where the orcs are hidden. He then explains how Uncle Joon, whom he is sure is on the good guys’ side, told him all about every Orc tribe and the best places for Orcs to create a den. 
Orsik stares at him and mumbles, “or you can fly up 50 feet and see them.” 
Thistle grouses and flies at an altitude of 7 feet, leading the group deeper into the forest and then towards some hills. At some point, he gets quieter and indicates with his fingers towards the edge of a cliff wall. But it is too late; an Orog stands beside the cave entrance and runs inside. 
Seeing the Orog run into the cave, Thistle proposes that they wait outside, ready to attack anyone who exits the caves.
An ambush is prepared, and time passes. And no one exits. 
Thistle decides to enter the caves to see what’s up. 
Time passes, and nothing changes.  
And then all abyss breaks loose.

The orcs had set up a trap deeper in the cave. That trap was dealt with thanks to the use of fireballs.
“FINALLY” shouted Orsik when the first fireball exploded. 
Unfortunately, for the one known as Unla Kee, in the hallway from where he cast his spell, an orog and an acolyte of Grummsh erupted,  and he was promptly knocked unconscious. 
Fortunately for his allies, Orsik, Wallace, and Thistle were able to kill the enemy. 
The group explores the cave complex and the dead Orcs and Orog. One of the Orcs was an Eye of Grummsh, a shaman of Grummsh. The acolyte was clearly a follower of his. 
The room with the trap was, according to Vardis, a War Hearth. There were plentiful shrines to the orc gods and accommodations for many orcs.
