And I will use this to get Hyuth to calm down because the next hit won’t just show up at my desk. And when he calms down, without murder, I will win in a landslide. I need to go talk to that shitty magic user right now.” 
Orsik intervenes, “We can do that. We’ll make sure he knows it’s from you.” 
“Tell him that I, the Lord Protector intervened to stop this incident. And that I may not be able to intervene in the future. And he may want to reconsider being a productive citizen and thus protected by the 12.” 
The group leaves, and she shouts, “Bring the contract back when you are done. I’ll need it for safekeeping.”
As the 3HPP approach the mansion of Hyuth, they notice its size, its opulence, and the missing roof that has been obliterated and has scorch marks everywhere. 
A whiny voice screeches, “Who are you?” 
“Here to repair your roof,” Orsik says. Thistle had suggested that they use that as an angle to get in. 
“I don’t want it fixed.” 
“Well, it’s free.” 
“I don’t trust you. I don’t trust anybody. Everyone is out to kill me.” 
“The Lord Protector asked us to come to fix the mansion. The roof damage affects property values, creating an eye sore, and she offered to foot the bill.” 
“PROPERTY VALUES! THIS IS HARASSMENT!” he screeches. Then pauses, “She offered to pay?” 
And with that, the group enters the house and sees a 55-year-old, tired man. He has a paunch, grey hair, and crow’s eyes. He stares at the 3HPP as they walk in and says, “Most construction workers don’t carry great axes, swords, spells, and a bow.” 
Orsik says, “We use magic.” 
Hyuth sighs and then leads the group to his second-floor study. It is a complete disaster area. Burned papers are all over, the desk is smashed, the roof is trashed, and the rain, wind, and sun have damaged whatever was left. 
At this point, Orsik turns to Thistle, “So what’s the next move.” But poor Thistle wasn’t very good at Seelie court intrigues and is unsure.

# Chapter 37 - On the Road Again
## Thistle Wendrod
Thistle Wendrod flitters about on the road to Xanthalar’s Keep. As he looks around for any information about his beloved, he reflects on the peculiar conversation his group had with the owner of The Triboar Travelers.
It all started when Orsik wanted to meet with the local Zhentarim agent. As Orsik was now a well-known agent, having acquired quite a bit of renown, Orsik wished to cash in on his popularity. 
The agent was Urlam Stockspool, of fairly conventional Illuskan stock, middle-aged, missing an eye, and caring his allegiance on the eye patch. The cloth had a red-emblazoned symbol of the Zhentarim. 
Urlam was quite happy to see Orsik and, after giving him a kiss on each cheek, agreed to go to the Talking Troll for drinks. 
At the Talking Troll, Urlam arrived with quite a crew. 
Thistle snickered as he remembered Orsik about to object about buying drinks for 10 people and then caving when he realized they were all members of the Zhentarim.
The conversation was going nowhere until Thistle asked about Adimond Kroskas. And then he replayed the conversation one more time. 
“Well, that is a name I haven’t heard in a very long time.”
Thistle fluttered closer, “we’re trying to track him down.”
Urlam looks at the fairy and has this uncomfortable look, “The black network is now all about making money and not trying to bring about the Apocalypse to enable Bane to take over all of creation. We’re a different kind of group. We are focused on profitability, gross margins, and endless growth. Adimond Kroskas used to work with that group.” 
Thistle is confused, “what would the Cult of the Dragon have to do with Bane?”  
The Illuskan trader scratches his head and continues, “Adminond Kroskas was not a member of the Cult. He was a free agent. He was one messed-up dude. People paid him to do things.”
At this point, Thistle is very interested and listens carefully to Urlam, “The stories that I have heard, and these are rumors and speculations, is that if you ever needed somebody to create a hybrid magical creature, he was your guy.” 
“Where can I find the people that knew him?”
“Oh, they are dead. And the people who may know are very senior in the Zhentarim hierarchy. The Zhentarim were involved in the events surrounding the fall of the Cult of the Dragon.” 
Thistle is frustrated, “where would I go to find any information?”
Urlam turns to Kaelen, “More wine.” 
After Kaelen returns with another bottle that Orsik has to pay for, Urlam says, “Silverymoon. They have schools of magic with the greatest libraries.” 
## Orsik
The dwarf’s ears perk up when Thistle asks about House Margaster. 
“Those devil-worshipping pieces of – bovine feces.” 
“We need to take them out.”  
Urlam swirls his glass, sniffs the wine, and says, “It’s not about taking them out. They came here and asked for a discount, and when I tried to object, I got these cultists to show who killed up and killed some of my guards. No made men, but still.”
“We really need to take them out.” 
“I wish the big bosses said they are untouchable.” Urlam pauses and continues,  “I know you talked to Orthovir, and I bet he told you about how to hurt House Margaster. Well, I know he has a relationship with those Margasters.”
Orsik is pissed off and very surprised when the normally taciturn, quiet cleric says, “He set us up.” 
And then Orsik is really pissed off when Thistle asks Urlam if he has any contraband he needs to dispose of. 
Urlam is furious, so Orsik intervenes, “We are having a nice conversation here, and you are bringing up this.” The owner of the Triboar Traveler is about to get testy, so Orsik shouts to Kaelen, “Another round.” 
Thistle backpedals, “We were thinking of using the contraband, that you don’t have to disgrace House Margaster.” 
“I just want them dead.” Urlam then pauses, “That’s not an official Zhentarim position.” 
Orsik smiles, “understood.” 
“I’ll pay you 200 gold?”
“I’d do it for free, but that is pretty low.” 
“Zhentarim friends and family discount?” 
“Sure, I mean, I was going to do it for free.” 
## Unla Kee

While Thistle and Orsik reflect on the barroom conversation while the 3HPP march to Xanthalar’s Keep, Unla Kee thinks about Hyuth. 
Hyuth is Unla Kee’s kind of guy. Hyuth is self-centered, obsessed with his importance, putting his needs above everyone else’s. 
“I like him.”
And then Unla Kee pauses, “I mean, I don’t – like – anyone….”
The halfling reflects silently, “I suppose if the world doesn’t end, I could retire like Hyuth. Big house, terrorizing people, being involved in various manic plots to destabilize the local community.” 
As he thinks about all the different ways he will arrange his house, Orsik, and Vardis declare that now they must rest. 
The fire in the clearing off the side of the road is warm. And Unla Kee is getting ready to sleep when out of the distance, a booming voice exclaims.
“It is I, Harshnag, the Grim of Force Grey.” 
Unla Kee readies his spells. 
Orsik shouts, “Harshnag, I know that guy. He’s a legendary good-aligned frost giant. In taverns throughout the North, he is known as a monster to some and a hero to others. In Waterdeep, he’s remembered as the largest member of Force Grey, a notoriously destructive adventuring company that came to the city’s defense on more than one occasion. Believed to be hundreds of years old, Harshnag wanders the wilderness with greataxe, seeking to lay his evil giant brethren low.” 
Unla Kee is impressed with Orsik’s erudite complete paragraph explanation. And is annoyed when Orsik notices that, and the fighter taps his headband.
The magic-user had much of his identity tied to the fact that he was the brains. 
Not that he was the most intelligent person, but that he was smarter than everyone else so that his evil plots would not be discovered. Orsik was going to make things – ever so enjoyable. 
Harshnag approaches. He looks like a typical frost giant except that he’s clad in plate armor and wears the skull of a white dragon as a helm. His jagged axe also looks quite formidable.
He sits by the fire and says, “I have been looking for people like you. The end of the world is near.” 
Unla Kee smiles. He’s the man with the plan. 
Harshnag continues, “The ordning has been broken, the storm giants no longer have the power to keep the smaller giants in check, and King Hekaton is missing.”
Unla Kee sighs and thinks, “Not the right end of the world.” 
Orsik is confused, and Harshnag explains that the giant world is kept in a strict hierarchical power structure that ensures that the giants do not meddle with the other races. 
“The power structure is often disrupted; when that happens, the giant kings wrestle for control. Their squabbles hurt the other people of the North. Such a thing hasn’t happened in a long time.” 
He then sighs, “I have been looking for you for some time. Krowen Valharrow said you had agreed to take on the quest to discover what was happening with the Giants. I have a proposal.”
Unla Kee stares at him carefully. 
“It might be time to consult with a higher authority. There is a mountain temple built by my ancient ancestors under the Spine of the World called the Eye of the All-Father. According to giant legend, the temple contains a divine oracle. I will guide you to the oracle so that you might learn what is going on.” 
Unla Kee is annoyed, “We were going to claim an award about a scumbag named Weevil who happens to be in Xanthalar’s Keep. Is this that urgent? Do we have to forgo that award?”
Harshnag stares at him, “Oh no. We can claim that bounty. But I will expect my share, a minor 100 gold.” 
The group stares and decides that the 100 gold is a small tax on their 5000 gold. A tax they can live with.
Orsik snickers, “Sure Frosty the Snow Giant!” 

# Chapter 38- Harshnag the Grim of the Legendary Force Grey
## Harshnag the Grim
Harshnag sits and thinks to himself. 
“Orsik and his sidekick Vardis keep calling me Frosty. I am a Frost Giant. Being called Frosty is disrespectful. You would think having someone like me on their side and offering them help to get the secret lost temple of the Giant god would earn me some credibility, some respect, but no.” 
Harshnag grumbles some more, “And then there is the halfling, the one known as Unla Kee. I have never, ever met such a narcissistic, self-absorbed personality. And a complete disregard for personal safety.” 
“Krowen was insistent that they were his agents,” he mutters inaudibly. 
“Little people”!
“And, of course, they object,” he thinks.
“Very well, non-giants, the Harper Agent Krowen Valharrow has said that you have accepted being his agents for hire to learn about the events surrounding the end of the Ordning.” 
Harshnag stares at Vardis, who is confused about what the Ordning is. 
“The Ordning is hierarchical order that the Giant races fit into. It’s a caste system that the God of all giants, Amn, created. The storm giants are at the top of the order, the cloud giants are next, the fire and frost giants are peers, and the hill giants are at the bottom.” 
Vardis is delighted, “so your rigid caste system ended? That’s great! Freedom!” 
The Frost Giant glares – “You do not understand. It is the Ordning that has kept the peace for so many centuries. Without the Ordning, the Giants would attack and destroy each other and everyone in between.” 
Unla Kee is pleased, “chaos!”
Harshnag sighs and remembers what Krowen said, “You promised and were given access to the teleportation circles, and I believe there was a quid pro quo that you would help.” 
He watches them slump.
“Excellent. Now, I know that although the Ordning is a religious imperative that all pious Giants must observe, it is also true that Giants are not all pious. King Hekaton of the Storm Giants has disappeared. And without him, the powerful Giant leaders have started to wage war. I  now think it is time to consult with a higher authority. I know the location of a mountain temple, the Eye of the All-Father, built by my ancient ancestors under the Spine of the World. According to giant legend, the temple contains a divine oracle. I will lead you to the temple so you can consult this oracle and learn how best to end the giants’ threat to the North.”
## Unla Kee
Harshnag the Grim agreed to a 100 gold piece share of the 5,000 gold piece reward for the capture of the brigand Worvil Forkbeard, also known as the Weevil. 
Unla Kee was very disappointed to hear that Frosty didn’t know how to make a castle fly. 
“So you are saying that Cloud Giants are more powerful than you?” 
Frosty’s reaction was awkward, he brought his ax next to Unla Kee, and the blade was taller than the magic user. Unla Kee was not phased. He had faced death; if he died, the world would die with him, and the world would keep him alive. 
Unla Kee turns to him and asks – “so, are there any good cloud giants?” 
Frosty breaks out laughing. “Oh, my god. Are you one of those short-sighted, short-lived species who think biology determines morality? There are good cloud giants and evil cloud giants. To be sentient is to have the ability to make choices. What kind of bozos would believe that?”
“Silverymoon,” pipes Thistle.
“One of those places full of the bozos.” He then pauses, “But I wasn’t laughing about that. I laughed about a cloud giant helping you get a flying tower.” 
Orsik sees an opportunity, “anyone you might know?”
“Yes, let me think, Count Thullen, the younger brother of Countess Sansuri. Thullen is a good person. His sister, not so much.” 
After two days of travel, the edges of Longsaddle are visible. 
Harshnag tells them much about the town, “This sleepy little frontier village and member of the Lords’ Alliance.” 
Orsik interrupts, “Mr. Grim, what’s your take on the Lord’s Alliance?”

He spits on the ground, “What they want to do is create a federation of rich, powerful people who rule with an iron fist preventing the kind of freedom that adventure is and want to tax us.” 
Orsik nods. 
“Anyways, Longsaddle, as you can see, straddles the Long Road, with rows of homes and businesses on either side of the trade route. Noteworthy establishments include a rustic inn called the Gilded Horseshoe and a friendly fest hall called the Gambling Golem, where card games and a local marbles game known as scattershields are popular. “
He points to the distance beyond the town, “See!” And Unla Kee sees horses and herds of cattle on sprawling ranches.
Harshnag must have sensed what Unla Kee was thinking, seeing such a defenseless town.  “Monsters or brigands sometimes mistake Longsaddle for easy pickings — unaware that a family of powerful human wizards, the Harpells, lives nearby. Their grand house, Ivy Mansion, lies west of Longsaddle at the end of a long, winding path. The Harpells founded the village but took no part in its government, preferring to live quietly on their estate. Throughout Longsaddle and the Ivy Mansion, magical wards warn the Harpells when trouble’s afoot.”
Unla Kee is excited, “Gambling!” 
Harshnag begs the group to bring him a pot of the famous soup from the Gilded Horseshoe. 
Unla Kee heads straight for the Gambling Golem and places a small wager as they enter the town. A gamble that he influences with his ability to see the future and adjust the fates. 
After a long night, the group continues their journey to their final destination Xanthalar’s Keep. 
## Lord of Xantharl’s Keep Narbeck Horn
“How unbelievably annoying,” he thinks, “Orgazol has once again accepted a bribe from some adventurer who believes they must talk to me.” 
“Sir, I am pursuing a dangerous criminal called the Weevil.” 
“So?”
“He is in your town.” 
“So?”
“We wish to arrest him.” 
“Then do that, and make sure you don’t arrest the wrong person. The Ax of Mirabar shows no mercy to those who arrest the wrong man.”
“He’s a dwarf.” 
“My apologies. I don’t take care of these details; I take care of the bigger picture. My job is to set policy. Why don’t you talk to my lieutenant? That seems to like you.” 
Narbeck smiles evilly at his lieutenant, who realizes these bounty hunters may not be the only people arrested if this goes sideways. 
Just then, a horn blows, and Narbeck is shocked into action, “we are under attack.” 
Another officer whispers to him, and Narbeck smiles. “A foolish group of goblins, hobgoblins, and ogres are attacking! A Frost Giant leads them.” 
The dwarf’s friends seem worried. Narbeck thinks,  “I will have to look into that….” 
“From where?” Narbeck overhears. 
“The west.” And then he turns to shout to his guards. 
The fairy volunteers some help to Narbeck when another guard shouts, “Another Frost Giant is attacking the first one!” 

## Vardis

“If I wanted to be a city guard, I would have kept the job at Parnast. Not interested,” he thinks.
And as he looks around for a way to exit, he notices a group of bugbears coming in from the east. 
“It’s a diversion; the bugbears are coming from the east. The Falling Orc, where Larg, aka Weevil, is hiding, is there. They probably are looking to secure the reward.” 
Vardis starts to run. Orsik seeing the money about to be lost, follows. Thistle and Unla Kee join the pursuit.
The battle with the Bugbears is explosive. Unla Kee and Vardis let loose fireballs that wound poor weevil. And then Orsik and Thistle finish the thieves with good old-fashioned violence. 
## Weevil 

“Thank god you saved me!” Weevil shouts. “I was about to be captured by these evil creatures.” 
The fairy walks up to him, shows him the wanted poster he picked up in Longsaddle, and mutters, “I don’t think so.” 

# Chapter 39 - Downtime in Mirabar
## Fred and George’s Report
Xaltivar’s attack on Xanthalar’s Keep was a ruse. The plan was to distract the main force of the guard while a detachment of bugbears jumped the walls and secured a prisoner named Worvil Forkbeard, who goes by the alias – “The Weevil.” 
We were able to thwart the main attack thanks to the support of a group known as the Three Halfpints and a Pint, or 3HPP. 
The 3HPP planned to leave Xanthalar’s Keep before the battle ended and demanded we open the gates. 
We stood our ground and insisted that they wait for the battle. 
The members of the 3HPP had a raucous debate. One of their members insisted that they leave right away because his lordship  Narbeck Horn would be duped by The Weevil and say that this was a case of mistaken identity.
We chose not to tell them that His Lordship Horn would know that this was The Weevil because of the distinctive braiding of his beard. A braiding that all shield dwarfs can recognize. 
Fortunately, the Lieutenant showed up in time and convinced the 3HPP to wait, and no further events occurred. 
## Orsik 
As the group headed to Mirabar on the Long Road North, Orsik kept staring at The Weevil. The dwarf wanted to tell him something.
Finally, unable to resist, he ungagged the prisoner. 
“I have treasure I am willing to exchange for my freedom.” 
“You are worth 5000 gold to me.” 
“Ah, but I know how to get a much bigger score, Orsik.” 
“You know me?”
“Who doesn’t.” 
“Well, at least I am not tied up and gagged.” 
Orsik notices Vardis staring at him and feels momentarily bad that his good friend, the elf, is a relative unknown while he’s infamous. 
“I know how we can isolate a caravan from the mines of Mirabar and rob it. There is a secret pattern that I happen to have figured out. All I need is a crew, and I promise we will make a fortune.”
The fighter stares at The Weevil and thinks that the other Orsik, the less intelligent one that had been a disgrace, might have taken that offer, but this Orsik, the Captain of the Guards of Parnast, Defender of Parnast, will not. 
He thinks how much he had changed from that day when the old dwarf called him a disgrace, stares at The Weevil, and gags him again. 
As Orsik silences him, he notices how everyone except for Unla Kee is pleased. 
## Unla Kee

Unla Kee is grouchy. For the last two days, he has glared at Orsik, who seems interested in going straight and making easy money instead of big.
To make matters worse, he can’t get close to The Weevil to learn his secret method of robbing the caravans from the mines. 
As he runs through his head the hundredth plan to extract The Weevil from the clutches of the suddenly very good Orsik, Harshnag does his -blah-blah-blah about some other important geographical location of the North.
“Mirabar,” he begins, “is the richest city of the North by far.”
At that point, Unla Kee perks up, and wealthy cities have rich people that like to gamble to pass away the hours. 
“It sits atop a knoll on the north side of the Mirar River like an unassailable fortress, enclosed on all sides by sloped outer walls as wide at the base as many city blocks in Waterdeep.”
The diviner smirks internally, and those stuck-up Waterdhavians never talk about this city. They like to brag about how their city is the most important and most prosperous and blah-blah-blah. 
And again, Frosty’s voice penetrates his reverie. 
“Defenders can fire arrows down from atop the walls or, in winter, pour water down them to make ice slides. There is no shortage of stone and weaponry. Even the docks have battlements and fortifications.”
Unla Kee can’t resist – “Can we go down the slides?”
“No, it’s not winter.” 
“Can’t you make snow, like a white dragon? I mean, you are a FROST giant.” 
Harshnag takes slams the bit of his battleaxe inches from Unla Kee. The eye of the handle is about the size of the halfling and says, “How dare you!” 
 Orsik sniggers, “That’s a bit of a racial slur.” 
Unla Kee shrugs, “No ice sledding?”
Harshnag the Grim stares at the halfling and solemnly explains, “I will say this once. The ancient enemy of the giant is the dragon. Between our two kinds, there can be no quarter. There can be no peace. Only ceasefires.”
The halfling can’t resist, “Isn’t that peace?”
“No. The dragons started this disaster between the giants and the little people. Tiamat tried to seize power and weakened the North. The Giant lords saw an opportunity in that vacuum and sundered the Ordning.” 
And this has thoroughly confused Unla Kee, “So your racial war is why we can’t have an ice slide?”
The Frost Giant glares and bellows and then continues his monologue, “ Visitors to Mirabar often wonder why they don’t see more dwarves, as humans make up most of the city’s surface dwellers. Another city lies just below the surface, and dwarves dominate that place. Underground, Mirabar is a city of lit residential caverns, superheated forges, foundries that operate day and night, and tunnels leading to the mines.” 
## In the city 
Upon entering the city, the 3HPP declare that they wish to hand over the Weevil for their reward.
The sergeant of the guard, a dwarf named Herald, is delighted and congratulates the group and their service to Mirabar. 
The guards of Mirabar are known as the Axe of Mirabar, who wore the crest of Mirabar (a deep-red double-bladed axe with a pointed haft and a flaring, flat base on a black field) on their shield or armor. The Axe army has almost 2000 well-trained soldiers that can protect the city from attack. 
The Weevil tries to convince Herald that he’s not whom they say he is, but Herald points to his braid and says, “Silence! You are the criminal.” His shouting draws some other members of the guard, and Herald smirks at The Weevil, “You wanted ore? Well, you will have as much ore as you want.” 
Herald turns to the 3HPP and says, “he will be working in the mines to the end of his days.”
Orsik informs Herald that apparently, The Weevil cracked some code. This intrigues Herald, who tells The Weevil – “we have ways of getting that information from you.” 
After handing over their prisoner and flush with 5000 gold pieces marked with the symbol of Mirabar, the group agrees to meet again at the entrance in five days. 
Thistle uses his time to learn about his lost love Laela. From the Axe, he learns that a fairy matching her description passed through Mirabar and headed east to the Lurkwood. From a merchant named Gale Wonderbrow, he learns more. Gale has an athletic, imposing frame, short hair, and a chin built for taking a punch. Gale promises information if Thistle helps him arrange an elaborate wedding. As Thistle is a fairy from the Seely court, he can’t resist, and the result is spectacular. Gale is so pleased with the result that he tells him of a tale involving a fairy matching Laela’s description, slaughtering some brigands and bandits. The story was that these brigands surrounded a caravan she was on, and then in a blinding flash of mayhem, everyone but her and a young man who was cowering in one of the wagons was dead. 
Unla Kee gambles. And he gambles his entire share at the tables of Mirabar. As a newcomer, he’s not invited to the tables with high stakes.  And it’s a good thing. Although his luck was good at first, over the next few days, in a desperate attempt to cover his losses, he made more and more outrageous bets and lost half of his money. He would still be gambling, but Thistle saw him bleary-eyed clutching his cash and ready to throw it all on one last bet. Thistle knew that look and dumped some cold water on Unla Kee as he muttered, “Worked for Uncle Joon.” 
As for Orsik, he decided to use his newfound skills in magical disguise to masquerade as a member of a minor noble family of Waterdeep, House Husteem, a house of landowners and mercenary company owners. They have a reputation as belligerent and forceful. It is generally agreed that they found their way into Waterdhavian nobility solely through the depth of their pockets and the military power they wield – it certainly isn’t through any inherent nobility on their part. The rough and tumble style of the House makes it possible for Orsik’s rough manners to be excused. |

As a member of House Husteem, he manages to get invited to parties thrown by the rich and powerful of Mirabar, including a dinner thrown by House Hammaver. They are a mercantile house of the first order and saw an opportunity to entertain a minor noble of House Husteem. Yip Hammaver, a 32-year-old bachelor of medium height and the strangest red eyes, befriends Orsik, whom he knows as John Husteem. 
Unfortunately for John-Orsik, he offended another minor noble, Tack Proudfoot Esquire, at one of these parties. That then leads to a duel at dawn. Yip told John-Orsik that Tack was a bit of a dandy and not a threat. According to Yip, Tack read one too many romance novels that talked about duels. His death would make things very awkward as Tack was betrothed to one of the leading houses of Mirabar, Clan Battlehammer. And Tack’s death would be avenged. 
The morning of the duel outside the city, Tack showed up dressed in the finest armor John-Orsik had ever seen, prompting his quip – “I thought you said he was a terrible fighter?”
Yip replied, “Yes, for a noble night of the elite guards.” 
Seeing no way to win, John-Orsik cast sanctuary on himself and refused to fight. When Percival realized that John-Orsik would not fight, he declared that honor was satisfied. He took off his helmet and embraced John-Orsik.
Yip, Percival, and their seconds were delighted with the outcome. Percival, so much so that he insisted that the single John-Orsik should be introduced to many eligible bachelors or bachelorettes of the various noble houses of Mirabar. 
John-Orsik smiled and left quickly.
As for Vardis, he finds a notice for a strong sword arm to deal with a problem in a building. Hork Gethorn, the owner of a meat pie shop whose face looks like a meat pie and whose size reflects his love of his product, needs help dealing with an infestation beneath his establishment. Vardis takes on the job and returns with a reward and some treasure. 

## Harshnag The Grim 7th day of the 3rd tenday of Flamerule

Harshnag sits under a tree some distance from the city of Mirabar, thinking. The war against the little people is getting worse. He has heard rumors of Jarl’s raiding settlements to the north in the Icewind Dales. If one of the refugees is to be believed, Bryn Shander was laid waste by a group of Frost Giants looking for some artifact. From the south, he heard that Hill giants attacked the city of Goldenfields. And then he learned that the stone giants had attacked settlements on the surface. That frightens him the most. The Stone Giants view the surface world as an abomination that must be destroyed. And finally, there is the story that the 3HPP said that the Fire Giants under Duke Zalto was scouring the lands for an artifact. 
“This is not good, not good at all.” 

# Chapter 40 - Heading into the Spine of the World
## Harshnag the Grim
The frost giant sits under a tree, pondering the events he has heard. To the North, the Frost Giant Jarl has been laying waste to settlements as he searches for some magical artifact that will allow him to turn all of the lands of the North into Ice. To the east, he hears tales of the Fire Giant Duke, who wants to build and repair a magical device that will give him the power to master the dragons. To the south, he has met refugees that speak of the mad Thane who wants to wipe out all the non-Giant races and has already laid waste to many towns near Orlbar and Llorkh. 
As for the cloud giants, they are being mighty secretive and hiding in their clouds. “And as for the Hill Giants, surely no one should take them as a serious threat,” he thinks. 
In the distance, he spots the 3HPP advancing toward him; he gets up, stamps out the fire, and smiles.
“We must go to the eye of the All-Father, now.”
“Nope. We intend to go to Mirabar Mines  to check out the mines.” 
Harshnag is stunned. 
“Look, if you folks don’t care about your people being destroyed, then Krowen Valharrow has allied with the wrong people.”
“No, we care, but we want to stop by the Mines first.”
At this point, the half-elf interrupts, “Where are we going?”
When Harshnag is done talking, Vardis tells him – “We could go south over the pass, or we could go through this valley and climb up to the Eye of the All-Father?” 
He scratches his head, looks at his map, and says, “Yes, that might work.” 
“So great the mine is on the way,” shouts Orsik. 
Harshnag sighs. 
## Orsik
A day later, Orsik arrives at the Mines of Mirabar. It’s not the mines but more like the picket fence and the guard post at the edge of the land that may contain the Mines of Mirabar. 
A guard approaches and informs them that they can advance only further with the appropriate passes. 
A very cold Thistle shouts, “I need to buy some wing muffs! I NEED TO BUY SOME WING MUFFS!” 
Another gruff-looking guard stares at Thistle and grunts – “Wing muffs!” Clearly, this second guard doesn’t think such a thing should be a thing. 
Orsik looks at the guard and then, doing his best – “Hey, I am the hero of Mirabar because I caught the weevil routine,” convinces the guard to get the Foreman, who in turn is convinced that Orsik has been properly paid for his services. 
“You got paid for your work. Now please go.” 
Orsik is aghast. He gathers his wits and asks with a hint of desperation, tinged with malice, “Perhaps you need a bounty hunter?” 
The foreman stares at Orsik, and something twinkles in his eye, “I may need it in the morning. Why don’t you have dinner tonight in the lodge, and we can speak in the morning.” 
As the 3HPP enter the compound, their weapons are handed to the guards. 
## Thistle 
Thistle is cold. The Feywild is not a cold place. Well, Queen Tatiana is cold, but that’s more about her heart than the actual temperature. And it’s a cool kind of cold, not a disrespectful kind of cold. 
He really needs some wing-tip warmers. 

As he marches into the lodge, he notices its size. There are at least 200 men in one vast and tall room. The room is heated thanks to several fireplaces lit with magic because there is no smoke. Everyone is eating ravenously, apparently having returned from a shift in the mines. 
Thistle approaches one of the miners, a dwarf named Fruf. 
“What do you need?”
“Wing muffins.”
“Wing, what?” 
“Wingtip muffins.” 
“Well, I don’t have any of those, but I have some extra socks.” 
Thistle stares at the warm poofy socks and thinks – “if only I had someone who could craft them.” And then he remembers that one of the half-pints has demonstrated a remarkable knack for making things. And he turns to Orsik, who sighs, “Fine.” 
## Vardis
Later that evening, the cleric of Corellon Larethian, Vardis, is interrupted in his drinking when another elf taps on his shoulder. 
“Are you a cleric of Corellon?” 
Vardis, whose relationship with the Cormiira, the college of the high-ranking priests, is so toxic that he’s not welcome in any temple of his god, is uncertain.
“I have a relationship with that god.”
The elf smiles and makes a sign with his hand.
Vardis, unsure about what it means, says, “Peace be with you.” 
At that point, the elf stiffens. 
Orsik turns and says, “he’s from Waterdeep.” 
“A progressive cleric of Larethian,” the elf spits out the words and then says, “I am not even sure you are a cleric of our god.” 
Vardis shrugs as the elf turns away.
Orsik looks at him, “what was that about?”
“Look, I don’t know how it’s with you, Dwarves, but Elves live a long time, and there is a lot of interpretation of religious symbols, influences, and rituals, and we don’t all agree. Some of us believe that the great war that split the elves was a mistake, and others think that we have not done enough to end the great war.”
Vardis stares at his friend and knows Orsik didn’t hear a single word. 
## The Foreman
“I need to get rid of these yahoos. I know the type pretending to be interested in helping. For all I know, they are agents of the Weevil, or maybe he told them things,” the Foreman thinks as the 3HPP approach him in the early morning. 
The one called Orsik is smiling. 
“So?” 
The Foreman stares and remembers how Orsik claimed he was responsible for getting Weevil to reveal the details of the caravan code last night. 
He had spoken to the Ax using the unique sending stones and learned that was an exaggeration. 
Orsik had told them about Weevil’s offer, but the Ax had extracted the information. 
“Well, yes, I think I have just the job for you.” 
“Oh?”
“There are crag cats a day's hike out.” 
The Frost Giant and the Ranger try to urge Orsik to drop this quest, and the Foreman is upset. “Those crag cats are rather annoying, and I need them gotten rid of,” he thinks.
But the progressive cleric of Lorethainen comes to the rescue when he points out how much money is to be made from their pelts. 
## Into the wild. 
The 3HPP head out of the restricted areas of the mines into the hilly frozen terrain that leads into a valley whose name is unknown to those who live in the warmer climates to the south. The valley sits between two arms of the mountain range known as the Spine of the World that blocks the Reghed Glacier from advancing to the south. 
After a day’s hike, the 3HPP finds a tent with a burning fire and a corpse. Before they can pillage the body, four frost drakes attack them. 

The battle is short and brutal. Three drakes are killed, and one flees. 
“No crag cats,” mutters Orsik, who had already spent the money from the pelts on fine ale in his head. 
But not all is lost because the body has a map with an X on it. 
Harshnag stares at the map and says, “I know where that is. It’s a bit out of the way, but we can get there, and it won’t add too much time to our trip.” 

# Chapter 41 - The Sign
## 9th day of the 3rd 10 tenday of Flamerule 
### Harshnag the Grim
Harshnag stood at the edge of the small village on the route to the Oracle. He could see clearly the signs that giants had been here. The houses were destroyed with rocks, and bodies littered the grounds.
“We must go.” 
The one known as Thistle agreed, but Orsik would have none of that. There was a treasure to be found. 
Harshnag sat down and said, “I will not help you anymore.”
Orsik glared and charged in with the rest of the 3HPP. 
Although the ambush was well thought out, the heroes defeated. 
With the Crag Cats dead, Harshnag again shouted, “We must go.” 
And again, Orsik disagreed. And his friend Vardis stripped the pelts from the corpses of the monstrous cats. 
“Now!” thundered Harshnag. 
And again, Orsik objected - “We need to see if there are any survivors or treasure.” 
Harshnag sat down and began to pray. He had seen enough. 
Off in the distance, two frost giants were approaching. He would not fight to defend the 3HPP from their folly, they would either survive the combat, or he would bring their bodies to Krowen Valharrow and the mage would find new heroes. 
He overheard Thistle plead with the rest of them to flee. But Vardis would not leave his buddy. And the crazed mage was convinced that he had nothing to fear. He could not be killed because he alone knew how to save the world from the end. 
The fairy’s pleas fell on deaf ears. 
Harshnag’s pleas were scoffed at. 
So Harshnag left the village, and he said - “if you live, then you are the right hero. I cannot interfere.”  
Soon rocks flew. The Grim overheard Orsik scream for help. And the Frost Giant hero ignored the dwarf and prayed to the All-Father. 
As he closed his eyes and prayed, he could hear the sound of fireballs roaring into life. The ground shuddered from the sound of rocks falling. 
He sighed. He knew that his quest was coming to an end. 
And then he heard the most surprising of sounds, the body of a giant falling to the ground. 
“Can it be?” he wondered. And he prayed harder to the All-Father. 
He then heard the whooping of the 3HPP. 
A few minutes later, he opened his eyes and smiled, “They are the heroes.” 

# Chapter 42 - Traveling through Middle Valley
## Thistle 

“Time to loot,” shouts Orsik. 
Thistle glares at him; thanks to his greed, impulsiveness, and insistence on fighting Giants, his opportunity to find the love of his fairy heart almost ends. “Laela is in terrible danger, and I must find her!” 
And so he stares at Orsik, who confidently walks up to the Frost Giant corpses, goes through their bags, and shouts, “HA! SEE!” 
His hands are full of gold and silver coins, with an unmistakable sound of more gold. 
“We went from being broke to rich!” shouts Vardis
And Thistle thinks, “Having more money will help finance our first home back in the fey....” 
His reverie is interrupted when Orsik turns to Vardis and says, “Imagine how much money we will make from the merchandising of the demon Bob Sagert.” 
“Merchandising? What’s that” he thinks. 
Vardis looks at Thistle and says, “Selling little dolls of the demon while the play goes national.”
“Time to check the buildings of this town. What is the name of this town?” 
He muttered "Prettyview" and gestured towards a sign that an unknown object had been significantly damaged. The sign was directed towards a sizable structure with gaping openings that boulders hurled by frost giants caused. The sign read, "Welcome to the finest Inn of Prettyview!"The name of the tavern is not visible. 
Thistle and Orsik look inside and are disappointed. The place is in shambles, and dead bodies are everywhere, crushed from the stones that fell through the sky or cleaved by giant swords. 
“No place to sleep,” shouts Orsik.
Thistle thinks, “And no treasure.” 
The next few houses are the same, demolished and empty. One of them has a man dressed in armor as if they are about to go and fight, and they might have if a boulder had not squished their body. 
Orsik 
“We need to bury the bodies. Can’t have them turn into carrion.” 
Orsik is pleased and annoyed when Harshang the Grim smiles and says, “You have grown, Orsik. You are not the dwarf I knew in Waterdeep. The guard that did that Thing everywhere he worked all across the North.” 
“Will they ever let it go? REALLY?” 
As Orsik steps out of the house, he notices that only two buildings are standing, one is a barn, and the other is a house. 
And so, he heads to the house. It’s a massive building, 100 feet long and wide. Inside is an enormous library, and behind a couch, there is a tiny, terrified gnome. 
Orsik observes that the gnome is of average height, with a crooked nose, scarred hands, face tattoos, and orange hair. The gnome appears to be both terrified and relieved. Yazmeen informs the fighter that the town was attacked by giants who destroyed everything in their path and left their feline companions behind. The gnome reveals that he managed to escape because he was well hidden.
While Orsik peruses the books, Yazmeen reveals he is the local librarian. Visitors from neighboring towns often come to exchange their books for those in his collection. "And of course," he adds, "we always offer tea. Would you care for some? I could use a cup myself."
Orsik declines, and Yazmeen continues as he sips some tea, “The barn is empty except for some cows. And it’s uncomfortable to sleep in; why not sleep here overnight?”
## Unla Kee

“I’ll have some tea!” the magic-user shouts, and he’s soon drinking the tea, which turns out to be magical. 
And then, Unla Kee observes as the new kinder, Orsik, tries to encourage Yazmeen to come with the group, but the gnome seems determined to stay here despite the cold and the loneliness. 
“I have other library patrons, and I can’t lose my books,” he insists as he sips some tea. “Some tea?” 
As it gets cold, Unla Kee is relieved that Orsik wants to spend the night in Prettyview and the nice warm room they are in. 

And he’s even happier when he doesn’t have to stand watch because he’s not very buff. 
Except in the middle of the night, a monstrous aberration that looks like a giant bi-pedal toad steps outside of Yazmeen’s bedroom into the library where the 3HPP are sleeping and hisses, “YOU RUINED MY BREEDING PROGRAM.” 
Unla Kee is awake, and a good thing, too, because while everyone else tries to decide what happened to Yazmeen, he notices that the Slaad is casting a fireball and counters it. 
The Slaad is furious and realizes he’s outmatched, so he hurls flame at the books in the library, setting everything ablaze, and tries to escape through the window in Yazmeen’s bedroom. 
Unla Kee stops him with a wand of the web, trapping him in the room. The Slaad is furious and casts more fire at the halfling mage, trying to break his concentration. But Slaad misses, and Unla Kee smiles. 
And then, a few moments later, Unla Kee is pleased to see the creature is dead! 
“Can we get out of this burning building” shouts Vardis. 
Unla Kee mutters, “Shouldn’t we check on Yazmeen?”
Vardis
“Fine,” says the cleric and rushes in, picks up the sleeping Yazmeen, and rushes out. 
Yazmeen is fine. And does he have a story to tell? 
“Thank goodness you came! The creature,” he shudders, "was from another plane of existence. The thing wanted to use the inhabitants of Prettyview as interplanar anchors to summon his kind here. The spell was almost complete. Fortunately, or unfortunately, the Frost Giants attacked the village while the spell was being cast, and disrupted the spell by killing everyone.”  
"I can't believe I'm still breathing! That monster was using me as some kind of portal, but the specifics are hazy. It threatened to end me, but sipping the tea appeared to ward it off. Maybe there's some magical property to that brew?"
Vardis has a eureka moment; he figures out what is going on, “the tea was only effective for a period. When you went to sleep, the tea would wear off. When it wore off, the creature could return to our dimension.” 
Yazmeen looks at Vardis and says, “Yes! That is it!” 
As the group gathered around the fire later that evening in the barn next to the cows, Vardis overheard Harshnag and Orsik arguing again, “We should go straight to the Oracle!” claimed the giant. And then Orsik discussed, “The treasure map?”
As he stared at the two of them, listening intently, he saw how Harshnag would look up into the sky as if he were trying to think of what to say and then mumbled under his breath, “The All-Father sent a sign.”

## Avalanche  
After the events at Prettyview, the 3HPP continued eastwards deeper into the Middle Valley of the Spine of the World. 
As they trudged, to their horror, they heard a large sound coming from the towering peaks on one side of the valley. 
“Avalanche!” screamed Harshnag. 
With only minutes before tons of snow collapsed on them all and nowhere to hide, Orsik and Unla Kee devised a clever plan. Unla Kee cast water breathing on the group, and Orsik threw fire bolts to melt ice. Using the combination, the group avoided any chance of asphyxiation. Orsik then proposed they use the firebolts to cut through the snow. As the group huddled to make that happen and waited as Orsik cut through snow, they heard a hearty laugh and saw the sunlight peer through an opening in the snow.
“I am so sorry for helping you, I know how you little people don’t want to be helped by us Giants!” bellowed Harshnag, who had managed not to get covered by the snow and dig 3HPP out. 

# Chapter 43 - The Evil Gold Dragon
Harshnag was grouchy. And Yazmeen was freezing. But Orsik was very determined to follow the treasure map. And since it was almost in the right direction, the rest of the group tagged along. 
Or, more precisely, no one wanted to walk away from that kind of haul.
The treasure was in a cave that happened to be the lair of an evil gold dragon. The dragon’s lair had a pack of crag cats hanging around the caves that tried to feast on the remains of that dragon’s prey. 
“Evil Gold Dragon?” 
A common misconception is that Dragons are good or evil based on a single attribute, the color of their scales. 
Like most sentient creatures, it’s more complicated than that. The real culprits are the two dominant religious groups, the Cult of Bahamut and the Cult of Tiamat. Historically, the Cult of Bahamut tended to attract dragons with shiny metallic scales. The Cult of Tiamat drew dragons with primary colors. Over time, metallic Dragons managed to breed with other Dragons that were metallic and had similar interests. And the same holds true for the primary color dragons. Over time, the cultural biases of the Cult of Tiamat tended to permeate all Dragons that were here followers. And an analogous situation existed for the cult of Bahamut. For most non-dragons, Tiamat is evil, and Bahamut is good. 
This is a misunderstanding of “goodness and evil. Both Gods view Dragons as the apex species, and others serve the dragon's needs. The difference is the approach. Bahamut believes that his Dragons are the dominant species so that his Dragons are the prevailing set of his Dragons. And that having the fewest number of enemies is critical. 
Tiamat also believes that Dragons will be dominant but is impatient with anyone who will get in her way. Tiamat does not care for how many enemies she has because she has unshakeable faith in her final triumph. A few more or a few fewer dragons do not matter. 
And so Bahamut’s Dragons are good because they seem to not want to destroy non-dragons right now, and Tiamat’s Dragons are evil because they do not care if non-dragons are killed in pursuit of their aims. 
Because of how Dragon culture works, Dragons tend to reflect the group they are brought up in. And so, “metallic” dragons tend to be good, and “primary color” dragons tend to be evil. 
But the keyword is tended. Some metallic dragons reject the philosophy of Bahamut and decide that they aren’t interested in playing the long game and living within artificial limits that Bahamut has placed on their freedom of action.
This Gold Dragon was one of those Dragons. He was a profoundly philosophical Dragon who saw the Cult of Bahamut's hypocrisy and decided to selfishly pursue his own goals.
Why did he not join the Cult of Tiamat? Because after millennia of primary color dragons and metallic dragons fighting each other, he saw that as just trading one hypocrisy for another and because of profound doctrinal differences, the Cult of Tiamat was not welcoming to metallic dragons. 
Over time, this Dragon would have found a different path for all Dragonkind. 
Unfortunately for him, the 3HPP were not interested in a deep philosophical debate on good and evil. And so, after a fierce battle, killed him. 

# Chapter 44 - The Temple of Annam
The journey from the Golden Dragon’s cave to the causeway that leads to the Temple of the All-Father, is blessedly uninterrupted and takes two days

## 3rd day of the 1st tenday of Eleasis 1492
Thistle notices fresh human tracks. He asks Harshnag, but the Frost Giant is confused. 
The causeway ends before a stone dome carved from the mountainside. Six 30-foot-tall stone pillars support the dome. 
Orsik declares, “there is no danger of collapse of this dome, even if we were to destroy the pillars. 
Vardis stares at him. 
“I know stuff.” 
“Apparently.”
Each pillar is carved with a wraparound bas-relief. Harshnag points to each one North to South and explains what they are seeing. 
“This is a hill giant lifting a rock above it’s head. You can tell from the small size of the rock and the large belly.” 
“The next one is of a proud and honerable and great Frost Giant cutting down a pine.” 
“Birds in the sky, and the bird brained Cloud Giants are flying with them.” 
“I tell you that the Storm Giants are deadly. Here you can see one tossing lightning at a ship.” 
“Well that’s interesting, a stone giant climbing down a mountain. They don’t like to be on the surface.” 
And then he smirks and mutters, “See Orsik, the Fire Giants are a plague on all of us. They could choose anything and they choose to represent themselves as slavers with dwarves chained against the wall.” 

And with that the 3HPP advance forward. Unla Kee is in a rush and dashes in when he nears noises. Thistle tries to urge caution because of the possibility of traps, but the diviner has other ideas.
Above Unla Kee’s head, Thistle notices massive stone blocks that are held in place by some kind of contraption over the hallway. It’s unclear what the contraption is or how to disarm it. Before Thistle can tell Unla Kee to stop, the halfling has run into the main hallway past the two massive blocks. 
And then just before the stairway, the group is ambushed by Uthgardtian barbarians and their pet white dragon. The barbarians attack from the ledges with their bows and hide in the alcoves. However, eventually the battle tied turns. And in particular when Harshnag enters the battle furious at the idea of a dragon in this holy place. 
And then the battle is over, except for the Shaman who flees into the temple. 
Harshnag stands over the corpses of the Uthgardtian defilers, turning to Orsik, “These are members of the Great Worm Tribe. They found this Temple and came to plunder it. They were foolish.” 
He marches up to the door at the end of the hallway and says as he pushes open the doors with a giant heave, “Only a giant could open the door to the Temple of Annam.” 
Vardis looks around, “so this is some kind of ante-chamber?” 
“No, this is an impenetrable fortress.”
“One that these,” Orsik points to the corpses, “tribesman penetrated.” 
Harshnag explodes. “Antechamber? Penetrated? Bah!” He points to the ledges, “You see those ledges if there were giants here, they would toss rocks, not arrows! And those blocks would have crushed you or trapped you!”
And then he goes on for another 10 to 15 minutes explaining how this is a monument to the tremendous giant civilization of Osteria, “40,000 years ago, magic-wielding Giants built this.” 
Vardis turns to him, “Wait a sec, the Toril is only 5,000 years old!” 
Harshnag rolls his eye, “One of those cranks,” and he makes the Giant gesture for Loon, “A creationist!” 
As they are about to enter the Temple, Vardis picks up an amulet from the chieftain and hangs it around his neck underneath his armor. 
And with that, the 3HPP walk into the Temple of Annam. 
Vardis stares at the statue and notices that the face of the All-Father is blank. Harshnag also sees that and says, “The sculptors did not know what he looks like and chose to not offend.” 
Unla Kee rushes to the far eastern edge of the room, where a giant portal stands. The archway is 40 feet wide and 40 feet tall. Six runes are carved into it, each inlaid with mithral, and a glowing mist fills the arch. Niches in the walls on either side of it contain empty, ice-covered sconces made of iron.

He runs through the mist and collides with a wall.
“Nothing to see behind here.” 
Harshnag glares at him and then shouts, “Look at the runes. We need to do something with the runes.” 
Orsik turns to Unla Kee – “You’re a diviner; surely you have some divination spell?”
The blank expression convinces him that Unla Kee does not. Unla Kee explains, “I know the future; what else is there to divine?”
Orsik stares at the runes on the wall. 
“From left to right, they are hill, cloud, fire, storm, stone, and frost.” 
Orsik turns to Harshnag, “Didn’t you recognize that one?”
The Grim stares at it and says, “Ah yes, that’s an old version of the rune. The current one is much simpler.” 
“I guess it’s been 40,000 years.” 
“Yes, that is 10 generations of giants.” 
The 3HPP stares at the runes and then glares at Unla Kee, who doesn’t have a single divination spell memorized or even in his spell book. 
Harshnag stares at the runes and has an idea, “That’s interesting; all of the giants have weapons, and.”
Orsik tells him to shush, “Let’s see if we can manipulate the runes.”
Thistle looks at him, “should we start with the Storm Giant one?”
“It does match the ordning.” 
And so the fairy flies up and touches the storm rune. 
After he touches it, a storm cloud forms over the center and spreads over the whole room. 
There is a deafening roar of thunder. 
Thistle shouts, “What do we do next?” 
“Try touching it again? Maybe that will turn it off!” 
Thistle nods, touches, and nothing happens. 
Then acid rain begins to fall. 
Vardis shouts, “Let’s do the bottom of the Ordning hierarchy?” 
Orsik, who remembers what Harshnag said, notices that the giant that looks like a paragon of a Frost Giant has no weapon and marches up to the Frost rune and touches it. 
A magical ice storm is triggered, hitting the entire group.
Unla Kee is knocked unconscious. 
Harshnag stares at the storm over the god Annam and shouts, “We should run!” 
Orsik looks up and agrees, “Let’s get to that ledge.” 
And with that, the group runs through the room. Unfortunately, the room is 480 long, and when Thistle touched the Storm rune, he trigged a Storm of Vengeance. And after the deafening sound and the acid, the storm triggers six powerful lightning bolts that kill everyone in the group. 
Harshnag stares at the bodies, “Well, shit.” 
He quickly picks up Orsik, Thistle, Unla Kee, and Vardis, dumps them into his satchel, and runs for the entrance to the Temple. As he runs, he thinks, “Thank goodness it’s three tiny people and an elf.” 
Once outside in the temple hallway entrance, he dumps the bodies on the ground. Harshnag then rummages through the stuff of the 3HPP and finds a healing potion. He stares at it and then shoves it in Vardis’ mouth. 
## 4th day of the 1st tenday of Eleasis
The following day, the group is healed. 
Unla Kee muses, “That was fun! Let’s go try that again!” 
They all stare at him.
Orsik turns to the group, “Let’s devise a better plan.” 
Vardis muses, “Maybe the problem is who touched the statue.” 
Harshnag mumbles, “Why are the weapons removable?” 
Orsik and Vardis stare at him and then say in unison, “It’s the weapons.” 
“Huh?” 
Orsik shouts, “You need to take a weapon and use that weapon to touch the corresponding rune!” 
“Brilliant!” 
“Yes!” agrees Orsik.
“But which one?” 
“I think we should do the hill giant rune,” suggests Harshnag. “The storm giant rune was deadly.” 
And with that, Harshnag marches up to the hill giant statue, picks up its 500lb bone club, and touches the hill rune; the rune glows, and then a series of howling magical mad voices emanating from giant distended mouths surround him. 
The Grim seems unaffected, and the starts to act as if under the influence of a confusion spell.
When the spell effects wear off, he marches back to the 3HPP outside the Temple and says, “Well, that didn’t work.”
Orsik turns to him, “Well, why not try touching the Frost Giant Rune? You are immune to frost damage.” 
Harshnag stares at him and then marches back to the archway. He touches the rune. The rune glows blue, and then above him, he sees a blue dot shimmer, and then a magical ice storm pelts him. 
A few minutes later, he returns to the hallway and grumbles, “That didn’t work either.” 
“Why not use the Frost Giant weapon?” asks Vardis. 
“Didn’t I mention that it’s missing?”
“Oh, right.” Vardis nods. 
“Maybe it’s somewhere in the temple?” asks Orsik. 
“But where?” 
After some debate, the 3HPP decided to head through the double doors north of the Temple of Annam. 
The doors open into a massive room with giant stairs on the left, an alcove with a statue of a stone giant to the right, a hallway heading north, and another set of stairs past the alcove also going up. 
“Let’s explore this hallway,” shouts Orsik. 

And so they head through a 60’ archway into a hallway. Fourteen 30-foot-tall archways spaced along the west and east walls lead to 60-foot-square chambers furnished with frost-covered stone couches that serve as beds. Some of these beds have tattered furs atop them. 
Harshnag sighs, “Beds fit for a giant.” 
As he marches down the hallway, Vardis, who is following him, notices in one room there is a crack in the wall.
“Hey, let’s check this out,” he shouts. 
The rest of the 3HPP agree and go through the medium-sized tunnel. Harshnag takes the furs on the bed and lies down, sighing contentedly. 
The tunnels are 10’ high and 5’ wide and dark. Thistle leads the group down the snaking tunnel until they reach an intersection. 
Before he can react, spiders hiding in the shadows attack. 
Thistle’s one thought is – “I hate spiders.” 
Fortunately for him, Orsik likes killing them. 

# Chapter 45 - Looking for the Giant Sword
## 5th day of the 1st Tenday of Eleasis

### Thistle Wendrod
Thistle hated spiders. Something about giant spiders confusing fairies and sprites with flies creeped him out. 
He was reluctant to go on ahead, but the idea of having the halfling charge up ahead filled him with even more dread. 
So he fluttered ahead, with his wing muffs, through the dark tunnels, which eventually emerged into a large cavern covered in spider webs with three obviously giant cocoons. 
“Yuch.” 
As he stared at the mass of webbing, one thing came to his head, “larder.” 
Orsik lumbered right after him and used his magic to burn through the webs that filled the room and reach the cocoons that must have treasure.

The first is the body of a goat that has been bled dry. Thistle watches as the dwarf slashes through the goat’s belly, hoping to find something, and doesn’t. 
“But they eat everything!” he mutters in disappointment. 
Thistle watches as Orsik burns through the webbing to the second cocoon. 
“It’s a dwarf!” 
“Any treasure?” shouts Vardis. 
“17 copper pieces, a deck of cards, and a set of dice.” 
“Meh.” 
Thistle wonders what the third cocoon will hold. Thistle flies up for a better vantage point and smiles when he sees a person that doesn’t look dead. Thistle flies down and hands her some berries. 
### Orsik
“My name is Orsik; what’s your name?”
The female human is clearly attired in the garb of a member of an Uthgardt tribe. Orsik stares at her intently and figures out that she belongs to the same tribe as the chieftain they killed. 
“Probably not worth sharing that detail, he thinks.” 
“Noori,” she says. 
Orsik notices how she stares intently and with great hatred at Unla Kee. 
“I need to get out of here and find my tribe.” 
“You came here to this temple with your tribe?” When she nods, Orsik continues, “We found them trying to get in, and they were all dead.” 
Orsik smiles internally when he realizes Noori does not see through the deception. 
After a few more minutes, the group agrees to take her out of the warrens they found and return to the main temple. 

When they arrive at the entrance of the warren, they see Harshnag The Grim sleeping on a bed, covered in rags, snoring loudly. 
Orsik mutters to Nuri, “Be quiet; this giant can be very particular about people in his temple.” 
Nuri smiles at Orsik and runs off. 
Orsik turns to the group, “Let’s finish exploring these caverns.” 
And so once again, they go into the crack in the wall, past the larder, and continue to follow the right-hand rule. 
After a few minutes of walking, the tunnels reach an opening to the outside. They are standing next to a gap in the mounting above the valley they came up on the day before. They are over 400 feet from the ground. The descent is very steep and treacherous. 
“Let’s go back.”
As Orsik head back into the tunnel, something hits him. “Hey, these are not part of the original construction. These caves were formed when the mountain moved and cracked. Creatures have used these caverns as their homes.” 
Vardis looks at him, “You know this sort of stuff?”
“It’s a dwarf thing.” 
### Vardis

Orsik’s knowledge of stonework is surprising to Vardis. He thought he knew everything about his friend but clearly did not. When they were friends in Waterdeep, Orsik was a gruff, dangerous, fun-loving guy who liked to hang out at bars and taverns and never demonstrated any interest in caves. 
“More magic ring stuff,” he thinks. 
Vardis moves forward carefully through the caves, passes several intersections, and finally arrives at another chamber. 
Before he can get his bearings, Unla Kee rushes in.
Vardis takes in the sight that Unla Kee’s light reveals. The floor is the same rough-hewn ground, but in the middle of the room is a single column that supports a domed ceiling. Curtains of the web stretch from the walls to the spider. 
Vardis groans internally when he realizes that Unla Kee will not stop running in and that whatever is in those spider webs will target the mage. 
But Orsik, to Vardis’ relief, was ready and struck twice with his green blade magic. The spider bites Orsik, but his natural dwarven resistance blocks the poison. 
Vardis, seeing his friend under attack, uses his bow to kill the creature, only to suddenly notice that a pile of spider eggs suddenly hatch and swarm under the spider. 

“Shit, the mage is going to be eaten alive,” thinks Vardis. 
But the mage strikes with a thundering clap, and Orsik finishes the creature. 
Vardis sighs in relief and then walks up to the dead spider. There he finds a frozen potion of climbing and an old axe with a spectacularly beautiful sapphire. 
### Orsik
Once again, the 3HPP emerges in the room where Harshnag the Grim sleeps. He pokes the giant to wake him up.
Harshnag yawns and mumbles, “Haven’t slept in a proper giant bed in ages.” 
Vardis and Orsik gape, and then Vardis asks, “Where have you been living?” 
“Finding a giant-sized bed in the land of the little people is hard.” 
Orsik is confused, “you’re homeless?” 
Harshnag is infuriated, and Orsik enjoys poking the Grim, “My roof is the stars, my walls are the trees, and my bed is the ground.” 
When Vardis observes, “Hey guy, you could have built yourself a house, and you could have slept in a hammock,” Orsik smiles because his friend is doubling down on their mockery. 
“I don’t like hammocks.” 
And with that, Harshnag gets up, tosses the pelt to the side, exits the room, and continues down the main hallway. 

As Harshnag walks north down the hallway, he stares into the empty bedrooms, muttering, “Nice beds.” 
Orsik rushes after him. 
And just as he catches up with Harshnag, he hears an ominous thump. All of the entrances to all of the bedrooms have been sealed with magical walls of stone. 
“What the???” 
A massive 40’ diameter sphere of solid granite materializes in front of Orsik and starts to roll south down the hallway. 
Orsik watches as Harshnag smashes at one of the stone walls and makes progress, but not enough. Thistle, meanwhiles flies above the ball. 
Unfortunately, the rest of the 3HPP and the giant cannot avoid getting hurt by the onrushing ball. 
When the ball reaches the end of the hallway, Orsik is dumbstruck by the fact that it’s coming back, but this time slower. 
He tells everyone to squeeze in the edge of the walls. And they do. Avoiding getting crushed. And as the ball moves past Orsik, he notices what is moving it; the stone giant statue that stood at the hallway’s south entrance is pushing the ball. 
The stone giant appears uninterested in the 3HPP, to Orsik’s relief. And the dwarf stares in amazement as the granite ball disappears into the northern wall. 
“Magic.” 
And with that, the stone walls disappear. The stone giant somberly walks south, finally stands on his pedestal, and becomes immobile.
“Let’s not do that again,” thinks Orsik. 
Harshnag, clobbered by the granite ball twice, agrees and mutters, “Let’s use one of these rooms to sleep it off.” 
Orsik turns to his friend, “Wasn’t he sleeping like 10 minutes ago?” 
“Is he old?” 
“I am not old. I haven’t slept in a proper giant bed and room in eons.” 
“I’m just saying, you can’t be a toddler if you haven’t done something in a long time,” remarks Vardis. 
Harshnag is annoyed, “I am not old by giant years. And a long time is long by your reckoning, not mine.” 
## 6th day of the 1st tenday of Eleasis

### Harshnag The Grim 

Although Harshnag the Grim told the 3HPP that he was not old, the truth was that he was. And the comfort of a bed, and a room built to fit a giant, was comfortable in a way that he hadn’t felt in a very long time. 
As he stretches and yawns after a very deep sleep, he says, “I haven’t slept this well in ages!” 
Then Harshnag leaps out of bed and shouts, “Let us find this giant blade!”
They quickly walk south past the immobile stone giant to some stairs north of the doors to the temple of Annam. 
One of the little people insists they go up the stairs, so Harshnag follows.
The stairs lead to another long hallway and more rooms. The entrance to the hallway has a life-size statue of a female cloud giant wearing an angry mask, her arms positioned in a way that suggests she’s casting a spell.
Harshnag watches with curiosity as the group inspects the statue, expecting it to come to life at any moment. 
Soon they decide there is nothing to worry about. Harshnag is about to head north down the hallway when the fairy stops him. 
“How about I fly and avoid stepping on any pressure plates.”
Harshnag agrees and watches as the fairy flies off. 
### Thistle

Much like the downstairs hallway, there are rooms with two beds sized for giants. Unlike the floor below, the northern end of the hallway has collapsed.
In one of the rooms, Thistle finds a giant bag. 
“How odd,” he thinks, then proceeds to investigate it carefully.
Inside, he finds a bong, a gong, and a waterskin. Thistle laughs as he imagines a giant engaged in meditation banging on a gong while he smokes from the gong. He’s about to leave the room when he finds a long and slender shard of ice roughly the size of a dagger, and within it, the rune symbol of it glows. 

# Chapter 46 - Find the relic, save the world
## 6th day of the 1st tenday of Eleasis
After securing the Shard of the Ise rune, the 3HPP head back into the Temple of Annam. 
### Thistle
“Excellent work, folks. I want to apologize for not really being present while you explored the warrens and the rooms.” 
They all turn to him, confused.
“I was spending time in my dreams with my dearest.” 
Harshnag groans, and Thistle chooses to ignore it.
“But now that I am fully here, we must be far more systematic in our exploration. For example, I noticed we didn’t explore the secret doors.”
He points to two secret doors on the west wall, one to the North and another to the south.
Thistle flies over and squeezes through the one to the North. 
Inside, he finds a featureless room except for a lever 12’ high off the ground. He tries to push it but realizes he can’t. 
“Harshnag?”
The Frost Giant looks at the lever, “If you could melt this ice, it would be easier.” 
Unla Kee starts to explore esoteric options, including Fireball and burning hands.
Thistle sighs and pulls out a torch, and lights it.
Vardis praises him, “Bless our ranger.” 
Harshang takes the torch, lets the ice melt, and then lifts the lever. 
A loud grinding sound is heard, and Thistle flies off to see what happened. 
“Yes, this opens the Portcullis!”
Then Thistle flies to the Southern door, and just as he is about to enter, Orsik notices a crack in the temple’s walls. 
“My goodness! Look at this crack! You can tell a lot from a break in a building. The stonemasonry is impressive!” Thistle then waits as he goes onto a monologue about stonework. He then proceeds to discuss the finer details of masonry, rock formations, cracks, and the use of magic, which he disapproves of because anyone can use magic, and it really dilutes the artisanal work, and that, as far as he is concerned magical stonework isn’t stonework. Orsik\] then concedes that a magical device can shape the stone. He concludes that using magic to make stonemasons more productive is fine, but using magic to shape stones is cheating and really about putting stonemasons out of a job.
The room to the south, like the room to the North, has a single lever that Harshnag once again raises. And once again, a portcullis opens. 
Thistle turns to everyone, “Well, it would appear we have to go through the southern doors.” 
### Unla Kee

The mage enters the door and sees a vast, brightly lit room. It is 240 feet wide by 440 feet deep, with a 200-foot-wide alcove in the center of the east wall. Six pillars, each 20 feet in diameter and 60 feet tall, support the vaulted ceiling. The walls are lined with niches and sconces identical to the ones in the temple. 
The room is warm, dry, and brightly lit. The source of the light and the heat is a raging fire within a bowl-shaped basin in the center of the chamber. 
Unla Kee marches over to this fascinating source of light. “40,000 years this has been lit.” 
Thistle thwarts Unla Kee’s attempt to conduct reckless experiments by rushing ahead and declaring, “How surprising. In the fey, we trap elementals or create a fire plane portal. But this fire is boringly natural. The fire has been kept alive by natural gas escaping through holes in the basin. It feeds the fire, sustaining it indefinitely.” 
Vardis nods. 
Unla Kee catches up to Thistle and takes in the tables around the fire pit. 
Arranged about the fire pit are six tables carved out of granite, matching benches, and two 9-foot-tall stone slabs stained with blood and grease. Atop the southernmost slab are neat stacks of plates and goblets made of beaten copper, all green and black with age. The piles of dishes and goblets on the northernmost slab have been knocked over, creating a mess.
And just then, out of the ground, a centipede-like creature erupts. Unla Kee gapes at what he sees. It’s a huge monstrosity whose body is blue, and extending out from the body are what appear to be hundreds of legs. But that is not what is terrifying. The head has winglike fans, and the mouth has thousands of sharp teeth. He also feels a kind of warmth emanating from the creature.
Harshnag bellows – “A remorhaz! I will take the ax of Thrym while the rest of you hold off the creature. Then we will run away!”  
### Orsik
“No, we will fight!” 
And just as he says that, the creature swallows Thuul whole. 
Thistle gasps. 
Vardis shouts, “Frosty doesn’t understand the power of Unla Kee!” 
Orsik nods to Thistle’s tactical acumen to fly above a table to give more space between him and any creature from beneath and is pleased that the arrows hit. 
“One arrow,” he mutters.
Orsik advances and hits the creature with a swipe from the left, then a slash to the right, then he pokes it with the point of the blade and slashes again. 

The creature is wounded. And Orsik can feel the heat of its body burn him. 
And just then, he feels a surge of psychic energy flow past him and hit the creature. He knows that spell; it’s Raulothim’s Psychic Lance. 
The creature is stunned in place. 
And then, a boulder lands on the ground missing the creature. 
Orsik mutters, “Right, now that you see we can fight it, you want to help, but not up close and personal, from afar.” 
And just then, Harshnag surprises Orsik when he charges at the monster. 
Vardis runs up to Orsik to heal him, but Orsik waves him off, so the cleric of the First of the Serdaline casts a sacred flame and summons a spiritual weapon in the shape of a long sword resembling Sahandrian. 
And the creature is now incapacitated because Unla Kee is a target for Thistle’s bow. One of the arrows hits, and the other misses.  
Orsik attacks again, braving the flames, and strikes twice. He then turns to Unla Kee and shouts, “Again!” 
“I can’t, but maybe this will work.” 
And he waves his hand, and little bits of ice stream from his hand and trap the creature in binding ice. 
Unla Kee smiles, and Orisk understands that the diviner has twisted the fates to get the desired outcome. 
“Attack my friends, you, a horror of the ice!” Orsik is delighted with the massive attacks of the giant on the immobile creature, seeing its blood splatter and its carapace crack. 
And then Vardis, his friend, finishes it with a swipe of his summoned sword. 
Harshnag shouts, “I knew you could do it.”
Orsik is about to retort when his friend responds, “Didn’t you just say we couldn’t?”
“That was me encouraging you!” 
“Ret-con.”
Harshnag stares at him. 
Orsik is equally confused and remembers how Vardis went through his temporal phase, convinced that time wasn’t linear and that it was possible to retroactively change facts that created a new reality, a process he said was ret-conning.
With the creature dead, and the sword in hand, Orsik and the 3HPP head back from the feast hall.
Orsik turns to Harshnag, “Alright, the safe thing for us to do is for you to go with the Ax of Thrym, and hit the rune while we wait here.”
Harshnag nods. 
Orsik stares aghast at Unla Kee, who is running after the Frost Giant. 
“Are you insane?” 
### Unla Kee
“To take no risks when you know that you will be there at the very end and if you aren’t, the universe will end is personally liberating.”
Beyond the thundering archway lies a hexagonal chamber whose walls rise 50 feet before tapering to a 90-foot-high apex. Standing in each corner of the room is a life-size statue of a giant holding up a heavy iron lantern with one raised arm. Each type of giant is represented: hill, stone, frost, fire, cloud, and storm. A cold, magical light radiates from each lantern, illuminating a giant corpse lying on the floor beneath a shroud of frost in the middle of the room. The corpse looks like it has been here for some time, preserved by the cold. Next to the body rests a giant, frost-covered Morningstar.
Curiosity piqued, Unla Kee advances on the corpse. The giant is wearing a gold breastplate. Underneath his body is a circle of runes. 

A ghost in the form of a cloud giant rises from the ground and bellows – “Why have you come here!” 
Unla Kee just sits there staring, incomprehensibly, because he doesn’t speak giant. 
Harshnag walks up to him, brings his mouth to Unla Kee’s ear, and whispers loudly – “Ask him how to use the oracle, ask him about the Ordning.” 
The ghost understands the question and says, “You are lucky.” 
Unla Kee nods, “Yes, I am!” 
The ghost stares at him, confused. 
“This is a true Oracle. You can ask six questions every day. Anyone who asks questions in the Oracle will receive a true answer.” 
The 3HPP immediately started proposing questions related to matters that are top of mine for them, like the treasure map’s location and a missing girlfriend. 
And just before they enter the circle, the ghost speaks again.
“I have a quest for you. The divine oracle told my father and me that a great upheaval would upset the balance of power in the world, allowing all giants to win the respect of their gods and bring glory to their race. The oracle told Blagothkus, my father, outright that he could never impress the gods enough to earn their favor, then urged me to step out from beneath my father’s “dark shadow.” My father was overcome with despair and envy. A terrible fight between us ensued, in which my father slew me. He has since retired to his castle to mourn.”
Unla Kee is delighted and worried, “so what do you want?” 
“For you to kill him!” 
“One giant cloud castle,” he thinks. 
“And where is he?” 
“Parnast. I don’t know if it is a real place.” 
Orsik laughs, “We know about that place.” 
Unla Kee then remembers the sight of the cloud giant’s castle that blew past them so many months ago, almost killing them. 
“Payback and a castle.” 
 Before this can continue much further, Thistle marches into the circle and asks, “Where is Laela!” 
One of the lights on the giants burns out, and the Oracle answers, “Duke Zalto has her.” 
Orsik jumps into the circle and asks, “What is this a map of?”. He shows his old family heirloom. 
Another light burns out, and the Oracle says, “Beyond the Silvery Marches, near the Ice Spires in the iron tower.” 
### Harshnag
Harshnag is aghast, “Now that you asked your trivial questions, perhaps we can ask how to stop the giants, or maybe you are comfortable with this war?” 
He stares at them and realizes that they just might be, and changes tack, “Look, there is a very powerful magic user who has chosen to help you and might decide that you were not deserving of his support and may decide that he should get rid of you…” 
Thistle looks at him, “Just confirming the accuracy of the Oracle.” 
Harshnag remembers that a sign was given and then counts slowly to 10. 
Unla Kee rushes in; Harshnag is horrified but relieved when the halfling asks, “What must be done to stop the giants?” 
“Find a magic conch of the storm giant king, Hekaton. Use it to visit Hekaton’s court. Root out the evil therein.”
Unla Kee walks out of the circle, turns to Harshnag, and says, “Yep, checks out, this is an authentic oracle.” 
Harshnag glares. 
Vardis asks Harhnag, “Do you know anything about Hekaton?” 
“Yes, but what is this evil?”
Harshnag realizes he’s in the circle, and the Oracle blows out another light and says, “The storm giant court is in the chaos following the disappearance of King Hekaton.” 
Harshnag quickly steps out of the circle. Vardis mutters, “Thanks.” 
“Where is the conch?” shouts Vardis, and turns to Harshnag, “most direct question.” 
“In the clutches of a giant lord.”
At this point, Orsik turns to Thistle, “and we know about Duke Zalto! Let’s ask about him!” 
Harshnag nods. 
And the Oracle sighs, and the last light goes out. And it shouts - “First, you must prove yourself! When human warriors came to these lands, they fought our kind and stole our relics, burying them in the ground. The humans built altars to Uthgar, their god-king, atop these relics and surrounded their altars with burial mounds. Go to these mounds, retrieve one or more of our lost relics, and bring them here as tribute. Do this, and your path will be made clear. There are many paths you can take. The more relics you deliver, the more paths you will have to choose from. Several evil giant lords stand ready to oppose you. You can ruin all of their plans by defeating just one of them. Which one that is remains to be seen.”
“What are we supposed to do now?” asks Thistle. 
Harshnag sighs, “I know what needs to be done.” He walks outside the Eye of Annam and sits on the main temple’s floor. 
“I know the names and locations of all nine spirit mounds.” 

He spends the next hour drawing a crude map that shows where the spirit mounds are located relative to key landmarks and terrain features. 
“I do not know what relics are buried at each mound or which giant lord is associated with each relic.”
